items.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
items.description:
Google's latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the AI-generated model, manually adjust sliders on it, or input different values to change the simulation in real-time.
When trying out the feature for myself, I asked Gemini to make a simulation of the Moon orbiting the Earth, and it created a 3D model with a few different ways to interact with it. Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Google’s latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the AI-generated model, manually adjust sliders on it, or input different values to change the simulation in real-time.When trying out the feature for myself, I asked Gemini to make a simulation of the Moon orbiting the Earth, and it created a 3D model with a few different ways to interact with it. Along with a slider to adjust the speed of the Moon’s orbit, there’s also a toggle to hide the line representing its orbital path and a button to pause the simulation. You can also zoom in on and rotate the 3D model.There are a few different ways to interact with the simulation. Screenshot: The VergeThis update comes just weeks after Anthropic gave Claude the ability to automatically respond with charts, diagrams, and other interactive visuals, while OpenAI added a feature to ChatGPT that allows it to generate visualizations of math and science concepts. Previously, Gemini could only generate interactive images in response to prompts.All Gemini app users can access this feature by selecting the “Pro” model in the prompt bar. From there, ask Gemini something like, “show me a double pendulum,” or “help me visualize the Doppler effect,” and then select the “Show me the visualization” button beneath Gemini’s response.
items.author: Emma Roth
items.titleZh: 谷歌的Gemini人工智能可以通过3D模型和模拟来回答问题
items.descriptionZh:
谷歌近期为Gemini对话机器人推出重要升级，使其能够根据用户问题生成交互式3D模型与动态模拟。这一功能突破意味着用户可通过旋转AI生成的模型、手动调节滑块控件或输入不同数值，实现对模拟场景的实时动态调整。

在技术演示中，用户向Gemini提出“模拟月球绕地球运行”的请求后，系统生成的可交互3D模型提供了多重控制维度：除了调节月球公转速度的滑块外，还设有轨道显示开关、模拟暂停按钮，并支持对三维模型的自由缩放与视角旋转。这种交互设计使得抽象科学概念得以直观呈现。

此次升级正值AI视觉化功能竞争白热化阶段——数周前Anthropic为Claude添加了自动生成图表与交互视图的能力，OpenAI也为ChatGPT部署了数学与科学概念可视化功能。相比之下，此前Gemini仅支持生成静态交互图像，新版3D模拟功能标志着其多模态生成能力的显著跃升。

在技术架构层面，该功能依托Gemini Pro模型实现，用户只需在输入栏选择“Pro”模式，提出如“展示双摆系统”或“帮我可视化多普勒效应”等请求，待系统回复后点击“显示可视化”按钮即可激活交互模拟。这一升级不仅深化了教育、科研领域的沉浸式演示场景，也为工程建模、数据可视化等专业应用开辟了新路径。目前所有Gemini应用用户均可通过标准界面访问此功能。

items.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
items.description: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]
items.content: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual environments to real-world deployment faster than ever. With NVIDIA platforms for simulation, synthetic data and AI-powered robot learning, developers now have the tools to build machines that can perceive, reason and act in complex environments. Check back here all week for coverage on the latest NVIDIA physical AI technologies. Smarter Palletizing With AI-Driven Reasoning 🔗 In warehouse environments, palletizing robots typically follow fixed rules — handling boxes the same way regardless of contents, condition or fragility. A project developed by Doosan Robotics introduces a more adaptive approach using NVIDIA Cosmos Reason. By analyzing a single camera image, the system can infer box contents, detect damage and adjust how each item is handled — such as placement, speed and grip — based on estimated weight and fragility. This reduces common issues like incorrectly stacking damaged or fragile goods. To build robots that understand the physical world before they ever deploy in it, robotics researchers and developers are building policy models powered by NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models (WFMs). Toyota Research Institute customizes Cosmos WFMs for their own world model to achieve state-of-the-art results across dynamic view synthesis, state-of-the-art teleoperation data augmentation and navigation world models. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/nvidia_cosmos_accelerates_ai_training_for_robotics.mp4 Mimic robotics takes a different angle with mimic-video, a video-action model that pairs a pretrained internet-scale video model with a flow-matching action decoder, replacing the static image-language backbones of traditional VLAs with video-learned physical dynamics — achieving 10x better sample efficiency and 2x faster convergence on real-world manipulation tasks. Together, both teams demonstrate a fundamental shift: robots trained on world models that capture physics and causality need dramatically less real-world data to perform reliably in conditions they’ve never seen. Open, Intelligent Robotics on NVIDIA Jetson: Community Innovations Powering the Next Wave of Physical AI 🔗 This National Robotics Week, OpenClaw running on the NVIDIA Jetson platform showcases how quickly open source innovation is evolving into real-world, intelligent robotics. From practical applications to innovative projects, the robotics community is building what’s next — and fast. Developers are pushing the boundaries of autonomy — including hardware-in-the-loop testing powered by Jetson Thor, evaluating camera streams from NVIDIA Isaac Sim and even building systems that can generate their own code to complete tasks. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/oss-on-jetson-models-nrw.mp4 In addition, OpenClaw now running entirely locally on NVIDIA Jetson Thor — powered by optimized NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the vLLM open inference library — marks a major leap toward private, low-latency edge AI for robotics. And innovations like the NVIDIA NemoClaw stack on Jetson are expanding what’s possible at the intersection of open source and high-performance robotics platforms. Training and Refining Movement in Simulation 🔗 Gennady Plyushchev, a robotics creator known as Skyentific, is documenting the process of building a walking bipedal robot, from simulation and design to real-world deployment — showcasing a simulation-first approach to robot development. By using NVIDIA Isaac based simulation workflows alongside NVIDIA Jetson for on-device AI and control, the project demonstrates how developers can rapidly iterate in virtual environments before deploying to physical systems. The result highlights a broader shift in robotics: using AI, simulation and edge computing to accelerate development and bring increasingly capable humanoid robots to life. University of Maryland Researchers Develop Robots for Complex Household Tasks 🔗 To bring robots into everyday life, researchers at the University of Maryland, recipients of a grant from the NVIDIA Academic Grant Program, are developing AI-powered humanoid systems capable of performing complex household tasks with greater autonomy. The project centers on building robot foundation models that unify perception, planning and control. Using the NVIDIA Isaac open robotics development platform, researchers can create photorealistic, high-fidelity virtual home environments populated with diverse objects and layouts, allowing robots to practice millions of task variations and safely test rare or complex scenarios. NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs for training large models and NVIDIA Jetson AGX Thor developer kits for efficient deployment on physical robots help bridge the gap between research and real-world applications. By combining advancements in generative AI, sequential decision-making and scalable computing, the work represents a key step toward general-purpose robots that can support people in homes, healthcare settings and beyond. Announcing the MassRobotics Fellowship 🔗 The second cohort of the Amazon Web Services (AWS) MassRobotics fellowship comprises startups being recognized for compelling industrial use cases harnessing robotics and computer vision. They will receive access to technical resources and AWS cloud credits. The cohort includes NVIDIA Inception members Burro, Config Intelligence, Deltia, Haply Robotics, Luminous Robotics, Roboto AI, Telexistence, Terra Robotics and WiRobotics, each developing technologies spanning humanoid robotics, industrial automation, haptics and agricultural systems. Burro creates autonomous agricultural robots for tasks like grape harvesting and crop scouting. Config Intelligence builds data infrastructure for general-purpose bimanual robotics to enable reliable two-handed tasks in real-world settings. Deltia provides AI-driven manufacturing intelligence that optimizes assembly lines using computer vision and analytics. Haply Robotics designs haptic control devices that serve as “steering wheels” for physical AI systems across industries. Luminous Robotics deploys AI-powered robotic systems for fast, low-cost solar-panel installation and maintenance. Roboto AI offers a data-analytics platform that accelerates robot development by managing and analyzing robotics data. Telexistence develops AI-powered humanoid robots and remote-controlled systems for retail and logistics. Terra Robotics develops laser-weeding agricultural robots to automate sustainable farming. WiRobotics creates wearable walking-assist and humanoid robots to enhance mobility and physical interaction, using training data from assisted products to train its humanoids. Accelerating How Utility-Scale Solar Projects Are Built in the Field 🔗 Maximo, a solar robotics business incubated within The AES Corporation, recently completed a 100-megawatt solar installation using its robot fleet. Developed with NVIDIA accelerated computing, NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and the NVIDIA Isaac Sim framework, Maximo demonstrated that autonomous installations can operate reliably for utility-scale projects. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/maximo-nrw.mp4 The solution improves installation speed, safety and consistency, helping close the gap between rising demand for faster time to power and construction capacity. As solar expansion faces ongoing labor constraints and rising demand, AI-driven field robotics systems like Maximo are helping accelerate infrastructure buildout, reduce costs and redefine how energy projects are delivered. Aigen Advances Sustainable Farming With Agricultural Robotics 🔗 To help regenerate the Earth, Aigen’s solar-powered autonomous robots are breaking farmers’ dependency on chemicals through precision weed control powered by vision AI. The NVIDIA Inception startup is building a new kind of farming system that’s powered by clean energy and continuously enriched by data. Aigen’s fleet of solar-driven rovers uses advanced computer vision to identify and remove weeds, dramatically reducing the need for herbicides. Farming has no standard environment. Every field is different — different crops, different soil, different equipment, weeds, growth stages and geographies. That fragmentation makes real-world data collection slow, expensive and inconsistent. By post-training NVIDIA Cosmos open world foundation models on their specialized data and harnessing NVIDIA Isaac Sim pipelines, Aigen is building the system that generalizes for millions of agriculture scenarios. On the ground, each rover runs inference using an NVIDIA Jetson Orin edge AI module to distinguish crops from weeds in real time. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Aigen_Element_Weeding_1.mp4 Using these rovers, farmers can grow crops more sustainably and profitably, using regenerative practices that heal the land and foster ecological balance.
items.author: NVIDIA Writers
items.titleZh: 国家机器人周：最新实体AI研究、突破与资源速览
items.descriptionZh:
适逢美国国家机器人周，英伟达重点展示了将人工智能带入物理世界的突破性进展，以及正在变革农业、制造业、能源等各行业的机器人浪潮。机器人学习、仿真和基础模型领域的进步正在加速发展，使机器人能够比以往更快地从虚拟环境训练转向现实世界部署。借助英伟达的仿真、合成数据和AI驱动的机器人学习平台，开发者现在拥有了构建能在复杂环境中感知、推理和行动的机器所需的工具。

在仓库环境中，传统的堆垛机器人通常遵循固定规则，无论箱内物品、状况或易碎性如何，都以相同方式处理。斗山机器人公司开发的一个项目引入了更自适应的方法，它利用英伟达Cosmos Reason推理技术。该系统通过分析单张相机图像，能够推断箱内物品、检测损坏情况，并根据估计的重量和易碎性调整对每个物品的处理方式，如放置位置、速度和抓握力度。这减少了错误堆叠损坏或易碎物品等常见问题。

为了打造在部署前就能理解物理世界的机器人，研究人员和开发者正在构建由英伟达Cosmos世界基础模型驱动的策略模型。丰田研究院为其自己的世界模型定制了Cosmos WFM，在动态视图合成、最先进的遥操作数据增强和导航世界模型方面取得了顶尖成果。

模仿机器人技术则采用了不同的角度，其“模仿-视频”模型将预训练的互联网规模视频模型与流匹配动作解码器相结合，用视频学习到的物理动力学取代了传统视觉语言动作模型中静态的图像-语言骨干网络。该模型在现实世界操作任务上实现了10倍的样本效率提升和2倍的收敛速度提升。这些团队共同展示了一个根本性的转变：在捕捉物理和因果关系的世界模型上训练的机器人，在从未见过的环境中可靠运行时，所需真实世界数据量大幅减少。

在本届机器人周，运行在英伟达Jetson平台上的OpenClaw项目展示了开源创新正如何快速演变为现实世界的智能机器人。从实际应用到创新项目，机器人社区正在快速构建未来。开发者们正在突破自主性的边界，包括利用Jetson Thor进行硬件在环测试、评估来自英伟达Isaac Sim的相机流，甚至构建能够自主生成代码以完成任务。

此外，OpenClaw现已完全在英伟达Jetson Thor上本地运行，由优化的英伟达Nemotron开放模型和vLLM开放推理库提供支持，这标志着机器人向私有化、低延迟的边缘AI迈出了一大步。像英伟达NemoClaw堆栈这样的创新，正在扩展开源与高性能机器人平台交叉领域的可能性。

名为Skyentific的机器人创客正在记录构建双足行走机器人的全过程，从仿真设计到现实世界部署，展示了机器人开发的“仿真优先”方法。通过使用基于英伟达Isaac的仿真工作流以及用于设备端AI和控制的Jetson平台，该项目展示了开发者如何能在部署到物理系统之前在虚拟环境中快速迭代。这突显了机器人领域一个更广泛的转变：利用AI、仿真和边缘计算加速开发，让能力日益强大的人形机器人成为现实。

为了让机器人走进日常生活，马里兰大学的研究人员正在开发能够以更高自主性执行复杂家庭任务的AI驱动人形系统。该项目核心是构建统一感知、规划与控制功能的机器人基础模型。利用英伟达Isaac开放机器人开发平台，研究人员可以创建包含多样化物体和布局的逼真高保真虚拟家庭环境，让机器人练习数百万种任务变体，并安全测试罕见或复杂场景。用于训练大模型的英伟达RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU和用于在物理机器人上高效部署的Jetson AGX Thor开发套件，帮助弥合了研究与现实应用之间的鸿沟。这项结合了生成式AI、序列决策和可扩展计算进步的工作，是迈向能够在家庭、医疗等场景支持人类的通用机器人的关键一步。

同时，由亚马逊云科技支持的第二期MassRobotics fellowship项目已宣布，入选的初创公司因其利用机器人和计算机视觉的引人注目的工业用例而受到认可。他们将获得技术资源和AWS云积分支持。这些公司包括英伟达初创加速计划成员Burro、Config Intelligence、Deltia、Haply Robotics、Luminous Robotics、Roboto AI、Telexistence、Terra Robotics和WiRobotics，其技术涵盖人形机器人、工业自动化、触觉反馈和农业系统等多个领域。

在能源领域，从AES公司内部孵化的太阳能机器人企业Maximo，近期使用其机器人车队完成了一个100兆瓦的太阳能安装项目。该方案利用英伟达加速计算、Omniverse库和Isaac Sim框架开发，证明了自主安装系统能够可靠地用于公用事业规模的项目。该解决方案提高了安装速度、安全性和一致性，有助于缩小日益增长的对快速通电的需求与施工能力之间的差距。随着太阳能扩张面临持续的劳动力限制和不断增长的需求，像Maximo这样的AI驱动现场机器人系统正在帮助加速基础设施建设，降低成本，并重新定义能源项目的交付方式。

为了帮助地球再生，初创公司Aigen的太阳能自主机器人通过视觉AI驱动的精准除草，正在打破农民对化学品的依赖。这家英伟达初创加速计划成员正在构建一种由清洁能源驱动、并持续被数据丰富的新型农业系统。Aigen的太阳能驱动漫游车车队使用先进的计算机视觉来识别和清除杂草，大幅减少了对除草剂的需求。农业没有标准环境，每个田地都不同。通过在其专业数据上对英伟达Cosmos开放世界基础模型进行后训练，并利用英伟达Isaac Sim管线，Aigen正在构建能够泛化适用于数百万农业场景的系统。在地面上，每台漫游车都使用英伟达Jetson Orin边缘AI模块进行实时推理，以区分作物和杂草。使用这些漫游车，农民可以采用再生农业实践，以更可持续和有利可图的方式种植作物，修复土地并促进生态平衡。

items.title: Alleged images of the long-awaited Nvidia N1/N1X SoC surface on laptop motherboard — board features 128 GB of LPDDR5X memory alongside 8+6+2 phase VRM
items.description: Nvidia's N1 SoC leaks yet again, but this time in the flesh, with potentially a laptop motherboard featuring the long-rumored chip. It was spotted on sale on a Chinese marketplace for roughly $1,400. The pictures show the PCB rocking 128 GB of LPDDR5X-8533 RAM, robust power delivery, and a decent I/O selection.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia's N1/N1X SoC has been driving the rumor mill for years at this point, but we've recently begun to see some leaks that point toward the chips finally releasing this year. The latest piece of evidence comes from Goofish, a reselling platform in China, where someone has just listed a laptop motherboard allegedly featuring the N1 SoC in all its glory. It's priced at around $1,400 (9999 RMB), but offers have been closed.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe pictures show a small motherboard that could be from a 13-inch tablet (similar to ROG Flow Z13) or, more likely, a 14-inch laptop, as it houses a large cutout for a fan. The N1 SoC can be seen on the right side; it's the biggest part on the board, flanked by eight memory chips and what looks like a robust 8+6+2 phase VRM setup. That beefy power delivery suggests that the N1 will feature a significant power appetite.Upon closer inspection, the eight memory ICs are SK hynix H58G78CK8B modules, totaling up to 128 GB of LPDDR5X RAM running at 8,533 MT/s. For context, Strix Halo tops out at 8,000 MT/s while Panther Lake can go up to 9,600 MT/s in flagship configurations. Apple's M5 series also supports 9,600 MT/s across the board, and a report from WSJ previously said N1 is designed to directly compete with Apple Silicon.Article continues below You may like Nvidia's N1/N1X SoCs tipped for release in first half of 2026, debuting on Dell & Lenovo laptops Nvidia's Arm-based N1X-equipped gaming laptops are reportedly set to debut this quarter, with N2 series chips planned for 2027 Nvidia's elusive N1X SoC leaks out again (Image credit: Goofish)Anyhow, this motherboard also has plenty of ports — HDMI, USB Type-A, USB-C, and a 3.5mm headphone jack on one side, but none on the other. We can see 2x M.2 slots for 2240-sized SSDs, and there's a shielded communication antenna in the bottom-right corner for Bluetooth and Wi-Fi. It's a fairly densely packed PCB that appears more like a close-to-final, retail unit than an early engineering sample.As for the N1 itself, we know that the GB10 Superchip inside DGX Spark is based on N1 silicon, as confirmed by CEO Jensen Huang himself. It reportedly features a 20-core Arm-based CPU (10 cores per cluster) and an RTX 5070-level GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores. It's being developed in conjunction with MediaTek, which handles the CPU side, while Nvidia is, of course, in charge of the graphics and software. (Image credit: Goofish)The chip is so significant because after years of failed efforts, Nvidia has the chance to reinvigorate Microsoft's Windows-on-Arm initiative with silicon that can actually rival legacy options. Qualcomm's GPU drivers have held back the Snapdragon X Elite family, so Nvidia has the perfect gap to fill here. Once launched, this will be the Green Team's first consumer CPU since the Tegra X1 in the Shield TV back in 2015.All signs are now pointing toward a Computex 2026 reveal for the N1/N1X lineup after it missed GTC last month. A DigiTimes report from earlier this year claimed that Nvidia is targeting Q1 2026 for launch with more variants to follow in the second quarter. In January, we even saw a leaked shipping manifest for a Dell XPS laptop featuring an N1 engineering sample, suggesting that Dell is at least testing/tested the waters.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe Goofish listing claims that whatever device this motherboard is from is coming in the second half of the year, too, bringing "consumer-grade AI PCs" to the public. The board is also mentioned as an "Nvidia N1 AI book engineering sample" and that it "should be used for tablet computers." The text is translated, but the general sentiment is still carried over: N1 is intended for both conventional laptops and hybrid 2-in-1 devices. items.titleZh: 备受期待的英伟达N1/N1X SoC芯片组疑似曝光——主板搭载128GB LPDDR5X内存，并配备8+6+2相供电模块。
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达（NVIDIA）传闻已久的N1/N1X系统级芯片（SoC）近期再次出现实质性泄露，种种迹象表明其可能于2026年正式发布，旨在为消费级AI PC市场带来变革。

**背景与泄露来源**
多年来，关于英伟达N1/N1X SoC的传闻持续不断。最新证据来自中国二手平台“闲鱼”，有卖家列出了据称搭载N1 SoC的笔记本电脑主板，标价约1400美元（9999元人民币），但目前已关闭交易。此次泄露的主板照片提供了关于这颗芯片及其设计目标的重要线索。

**主板设计与硬件配置**
泄露照片显示，这是一块尺寸较小的主板，可能用于13英寸平板（类似ROG Flow Z13）或更可能用于14英寸笔记本电脑，因为它预留了大尺寸风扇开孔。主板的右侧可见N1 SoC芯片，它是板上最大的组件，两侧环绕着八颗内存芯片，并配备了看似非常扎实的8+6+2相VRM供电模组，这暗示了N1芯片将拥有较高的功耗需求。

具体硬件细节包括：
- **内存**：八颗SK海力士H58G78CK8B内存模块，总计提供高达128GB的LPDDR5X内存，运行速度为8533 MT/s。作为对比，AMD的Strix Halo最高支持8000 MT/s，英特尔下一代Panther Lake旗舰配置可达9600 MT/s，而苹果M5系列也全线支持9600 MT/s。《华尔街日报》此前曾报道，N1的设计目标正是直接与苹果自研芯片（Apple Silicon）竞争。
- **扩展与接口**：主板配备了HDMI、USB Type-A、USB-C和3.5mm耳机插孔（位于一侧），并提供了两个支持2240尺寸的M.2 SSD插槽。右下角还有一个带屏蔽的通信天线区域，用于蓝牙和Wi-Fi。其PCB布局密集，看起来更接近最终零售版本，而非早期工程样品。

**芯片规格与核心技术**
根据已知信息，用于DGX Spark超级计算机的GB10超级芯片正是基于N1硅片，这一点已得到英伟达CEO黄仁勋的证实。据报道，N1 SoC集成了：
- **CPU**：基于Arm架构的20核CPU（采用每集群10核心的设计）。
- **GPU**：性能相当于RTX 5070级别的GPU，拥有6144个CUDA核心。
该芯片由英伟达与联发科（MediaTek）联合开发，其中联发科负责CPU部分，而英伟达则主导图形处理单元和软件生态。

**市场意义与竞争定位**
这款芯片之所以备受关注，是因为在经过多年尝试后，英伟达有望凭借一颗真正能与传统x86选项匹敌的芯片，重振微软的“Windows-on-Arm”计划。此前，高通（Qualcomm）Snapdragon X Elite系列因GPU驱动问题受到制约，这为英伟达填补市场空白提供了绝佳机会。一旦发布，这将是英伟达自2015年Shield TV搭载Tegra X1以来，再次推出面向消费级的CPU产品。

**发布时间与合作伙伴**
目前所有线索均指向英伟达将在2026年台北国际电脑展（Computex 2026）上正式发布N1/N1X系列产品线，此前它已错过上个月的GTC大会。今年早些时候的DigiTimes报告声称，英伟达目标在2026年第一季度首发，更多变体将在第二季度跟进。一月份曾有一份泄露的货运清单显示，戴尔（Dell）一款XPS笔记本电脑搭载了N1工程样品，这表明戴尔至少已在对其进行测试。

**应用场景**
闲鱼列表中的描述提到，搭载该主板的设备将于今年下半年面世，旨在向公众推出“消费级AI PC”。该主板被描述为“Nvidia N1 AI book工程样品”，并提及“应用于平板电脑”。虽然文字经过翻译，但核心信息明确：N1芯片的目标设备既包括传统笔记本电脑，也涵盖混合形态的二合一设备。

综上所述，英伟达N1 SoC的泄露揭示了其在高端移动计算市场的雄心，凭借强大的Arm CPU、高性能GPU以及对高速内存的支持，意图在AI PC时代与苹果、高通和传统x86阵营展开直接竞争。其最终表现将取决于实际的能效、软件优化及合作伙伴的终端产品。

items.title: Intel and Google announce multi-year chip deal — Google will deploy Intel Xeon with custom IPUs for next-gen AI, cloud infrastructure
items.description: Although Google now has its own Arm-based Axion CPUs, Intel's Xeon processors with custom IPUs will continue to be used for AI and other demanding workloads in Google's data centers for years.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel and Google on Thursday announced a multi-year collaboration under which Google will continue deploying Intel Xeon platforms for its next generation of AI and cloud infrastructure. These platforms will rely not only on Intel's upcoming Xeon CPUs, but also on custom infrastructure processing units (IPUs) co-designed by Intel and Google. The announcement comes amid the accelerating adoption of custom Arm-based processors for AI workloads."Scaling AI requires more than accelerators - it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand," said Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Intel.Google currently employs Intel Xeon 5 and Intel Xeon 6 processors for a variety of workloads, including large-scale AI training coordination, latency-sensitive inference, and general-purpose computing. For example, Intel's latest Xeon CPUs power C4 and N4 instances. Although Google's custom Armv9-based Axion processors provide the cloud giant more control and efficiency at lower cost, many workloads that are run in Google's data centers need to either be backwards compatible with x86 or just need maximum single-thread performance offered by Intel Xeon CPUs. This is something that is expected to continue for years to come, which is why the two companies inked the deal.Article continues below You may like Report claims Arm chips will power 90% of AI servers based on custom processors in 2029 Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging Intel Xeon 6 selected as host CPU for Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems In a bid to make Intel Xeon platforms more efficient and suitable for its hyperscale data centers, Google will also co-develop custom IPUs together with Intel to offload networking, storage, and security functions from host CPUs. Ultimately, Intel Xeon platforms will combine x86 architecture with high single-thread performance and custom-built infrastructure processing, which will make them more competitive in Google's highly customized environments."CPUs and infrastructure acceleration remain a cornerstone of AI systems — from training orchestration to inference and deployment," said Amin Vahdat, SVP & Chief Technologist, AI Infrastructure, Google.The announcement comes at a time when hyperscalers and AI platform developers are accelerating the adoption of their own custom CPUs based on the Arm instruction set architecture. Just a week ago, Counterpoint Research released a note claiming that 90% of AI servers running custom-silicon processors will rely on the Arm ISA, leaving x86 and RISC-V about 10%. The announcement by Intel and Google clearly states that Xeon CPUs with custom IPUs will continue to be used for AI and other demanding workloads for years to come, which is something to be expected anyway.Intel's Xeon processors have powered cloud infrastructure since its inception in the 2000s, and Google's own servers before that, so x86 in general and Xeon in particular will not leave Google's data center premises any time soon. Nonetheless, the announcement clearly reemphasizes the relevance of Intel's Xeon CPUs, and when such a message comes from Google — which has been deploying special-purpose custom accelerators for years across virtually all of its services — it gets amplified significantly.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors"Intel has been a trusted partner for nearly two decades, and their Xeon roadmap gives us confidence that we can continue to meet the growing performance and efficiency demands of our workloads," Vahdat added. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 英特尔与谷歌达成多年芯片协议——将为下一代AI和云端设施部署配备定制IPU的英特尔至强处理器
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔与谷歌于近期宣布达成一项多年期合作，谷歌将在其下一代人工智能与云基础设施中继续部署英特尔至强平台。该平台不仅将搭载英特尔即将推出的至强中央处理器，还将采用双方共同设计的定制基础设施处理器（IPU）。这一合作背景正值行业加速采用基于Arm架构的定制处理器处理人工智能负载的趋势。

英特尔首席执行官Pat Gelsinger与谷歌人工智能基础设施高级副总裁兼首席技术官Amin Vahdat均强调，扩展人工智能不仅需要加速器，更需要平衡的系统。CPU与IPU对于满足现代人工智能工作负载对性能、效率与灵活性的需求至关重要。谷歌目前已在多种工作负载中使用英特尔至强5和至强6处理器，包括大规模人工智能训练协调、延迟敏感型推理及通用计算。例如，英特尔的至强处理器为谷歌云的C4和N4实例提供支持。

尽管谷歌基于Armv9架构的自研Axion处理器为其云服务带来了更高的控制力、更低的成本与能效，但其数据中心内仍有大量工作负载需要保持与x86架构的向后兼容性，或依赖英特尔至强处理器所提供的极致单线程性能。这种需求预计将持续多年，这是双方达成此项协议的核心原因。

为了进一步提升英特尔至强平台在超大规模数据中心中的效率与适用性，谷歌将与英特尔共同开发定制IPU，将网络、存储及安全功能从主CPU中卸载。最终，英特尔至强平台将结合x86架构的高单线程性能与定制化的基础设施处理能力，从而在谷歌高度定制化的环境中保持竞争力。

此消息发布之际，超大规模云服务商和人工智能平台开发商正加速采用基于Arm指令集架构的自研定制CPU。市场研究机构Counterpoint Research近期发布报告预测，到2029年，运行定制芯片的人工智能服务器中将有90%采用Arm架构，x86与RISC-V架构合计仅占约10%。英特尔与谷歌的联合声明则明确指出，配备定制IPU的至强CPU在未来数年仍将继续用于人工智能及其他高要求工作负载。

自21世纪初以来，英特尔至强处理器一直是云基础设施的基石，更早之前也已应用于谷歌的服务器。因此，x86架构，特别是至强处理器，不会在短期内退出谷歌的数据中心。此次合作公告再次明确了英特尔至强CPU在当下人工智能时代的相关性。尤其当这一肯定来自长期在其几乎所有服务中部署专用定制加速器的谷歌时，其信号意义被显著放大。

谷歌方面表示，英特尔是近二十年来值得信赖的合作伙伴，其至强处理器路线图使谷歌有信心持续满足工作负载对性能和效率日益增长的需求。这项合作也预示着，在未来异构计算架构中，通用CPU与定制加速单元（如IPU）的协同设计将成为满足复杂、多样化云与人工智能负载的关键模式。

items.title: Intel's EMIB-T packaging technology set for fab rollout this year — as TSMC CoWoS capacity remains limited,EMIB-T is preparing for advanced AI accelerator designs
items.description: If these “billions per year” in deals close, it’d mark quite the turnaround for Intel Foundry, which generated just $307 million in external revenue last year.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel CFO Dave Zinsner told the Morgan Stanley TMT conference last month that Intel Foundry is "close to closing some deals that are in the billions per year in terms of revenue" on advanced packaging alone, with EMIB-T, the next-generation variant of Intel's embedded bridge technology, driving customer interest as TSMC's CoWoS-L capacity remains structurally oversubscribed.That tech, EMIB-T, which adds through silicon vias (TSVs) to the bridge, is expected to enter production fab rollout this year and addresses the limitations that have kept standard EMIB out of high-power AI accelerator sockets: HBM 4 class power delivery and large package scaling.If these “billions per year” in deals close, it’d mark quite the turnaround for Intel Foundry, which generated just $307 million in external revenue last year against a $10.3 billion operating loss. Given that 18A isn’t expected to reach industry-standard yields until next year at the earliest, packaging is the fastest on-ramp for Intel, and EMIB-T is what could convert that into AI accelerator revenue. Intel Foundry's most recent announcement aligns well with this, having signed up to join Musk's Terafab project, though the reality of the effort delivering on all that's been promised remains extremely optimistic, as we've broken down.Article continues below You may like Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push Intel shows off leading-edge tech with massive AI processor test vehicle EMIB-T and PSVs (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Standard EMIB, which has been in volume production since 2017, embeds small silicon bridge dies in cavities within an organic substrate to route signals horizontally between adjacent chiplets, avoiding the cost and reticle limits of a full silicon interposer.Intel deliberately skipped through-silicon vias entirely with EMIB, keeping the bridge die simple and cheap. That meant power had to be routed around the bridge via the organic substrate in long, resistive paths, limiting the current that could be delivered to the dies above. That ceiling was fine for the likes of Sapphire Rapids and Ponte Vecchio, but not for HBM4-class accelerators.EMIB-T reverses that decision by adding through-silicon vias (TSVs) to the bridge die. In doing so, EMIB-T enables vertical power delivery directly through the bridge, integrates Metal-Insulator-Metal capacitors for noise suppression, and adds a copper ground plane grid for signal isolation.Dr. Rahul Manepalli, Intel Fellow and VP of Substrate Packaging Development, gave us a look into the full specifications of EMIB-T at last May’s Electronic Components Technology Conference — a 45-micron bump pitch with a roadmap to 35- and 25-micron, energy efficiency of around 0.25 pJ/bit, and UCle-A interfaces running at 32 Gb/s of data transfer or higher per pin. EMIB-T supports HBM3, HBM3E, HBM4, and future HBM5 stacks, and scales to a 120mm x 180mm package supporting more than 38 bridges and over 12 reticle-sized dies.Intel then followed up just before Christmas with a conceptual 2.5D/3D package integrating 16 compute elements across eight base dies, 24 HBM5 stacks, and a 10,296 mm2 silicon footprint, scaling to 12x reticle size and beating TSMC's planned 9.5x CoWoS-L ceiling. For comparison, TSMC's CoWoS-L roadmap targets 5.5x reticle this year and 9.5x by 2027, while Intel is targeting 8x reticle in 2026 and 12x or more by 2028.Bernstein analysts estimate a substantial cost differential, with EMIB packaging running in the low hundreds of dollars per chip versus an estimated $900 to $1,000 for CoWoS on a Rubin-class accelerator, according to figures cited by Investing.com in recent packaging analysis. Intel also claims roughly 90% wafer utilization for bridge dies versus approximately 60% for large interposers.CoWoS-L bottlenecks (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)TSMC has expanded CoWoS capacity from approximately 35,000 wafers per month at the end of 2024 to roughly 80,000 by the end of 2025, with a target of around 130,000 by the end of this year through new facilities at AP7 in Chiayi and AP8 in Tainan. As a result of these aggressive ramp-ups, Counterpoint Research projects industry-wide advanced packaging capacity could expand by roughly 80% year-over-year in 2026. What to read next Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' Apple and Nvidia considering Intel for 2028 chip production, report claims AI chip design is pushing advanced chip packaging to its limits Nvidia secured more than 60% of TSMC's total CoWoS capacity for 2025 and 2026, with every Blackwell GPU and the upcoming Rubin architecture requiring CoWoS-L. At the back-end of 2025, it was reported that TSMC’s CoWoS capacity was stretched due to AI demand, and that Intel was picking up packaging interest from firms either blocked from accessing CoWoS or looking for a shorter path to production.TrendForce then reported in early December that both CoWoS-L and CoWoS-S were fully booked, and that Google had cut its 2026 TPU target by 1 million units due to allocation limits. TSMC CEO C.C. Wei, of course, said during the Q3 2025 earnings call that AI-related capacity, both front-end and back-end, remained very tight, falling “about three times short” of AI demand.Only a handful of customers have been publicly named as being interested in EMIB-T, and there’s been no official confirmation by Intel to date. MediaTek was named by the Commercial Times back in November as actively recruiting engineers with EMIB experience, with the publication adding that Intel was “tapping Intel’s advanced EMIB-T packaging” to secure more capacity. Amazon is reportedly another named customer, claiming Intel is in active discussions with AWS for Trainium-class custom AI processor packaging.Meanwhile, standard EMIB has the customer pipeline that EMIB-T doesn’t. Nvidia's $5 billion September 2025 investment in Intel includes confirmed use of EMIB and Foveros, with Jensen Huang and Lip-Bu Tan naming both technologies on the joint announcement call, and DigiTimes has reported that approximately 25% of Feynman GPU packaging will run through Intel. Microsoft's Maia AI accelerator is Intel Foundry's marquee 18A customer under a $15 billion lifetime contract originally announced at Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2024, while Google is understood to be designing EMIB into its 2027 TPU v9.Intel’s Foundry VP of Packaging, Mark Gardner, told EE Times way back in March last year that Intel is "in production today and have been in production even last year in cases where we took things that were designed for CoWoS and are now designed for manufacture with either EMIB or Foveros," confirming that live design migration off TSMC is already occurring on the standard EMIB platform.EMIB-T for Jaguar Shores?Clearwater Forest, the 288-core Xeon 6+ E-core server processor formally launched in H1 2026 after a delay from its original 2025 target, uses second-generation standard EMIB combined with Foveros Direct 3D hybrid bonding across a 17-tile package. The configuration spans 12 compute tiles on Intel 18A, three base tiles on Intel 3, and two I/O tiles on Intel 7, connected by 12 EMIB bridges. Diamond Rapids, the Xeon 7 P-core server processor with up to 192 cores, also targets H2 2026 using standard EMIB plus Foveros.Jaguar Shores, the successor to the canceled Falcon Shores AI accelerator, is the likely first Intel product to use EMIB-T. A leaked test chip showed a 92.5mm x 92.5mm package with four compute tiles and eight HBM4 interfaces on Intel 18A. That HBM4 integration is exactly the use case that standard EMIB will struggle with, making Jaguar Shores the natural first home for the variant. Panther Lake, Intel's first 18A client processor shipping in volume since late 2025, uses Foveros-S 2.5D rather than any EMIB variant.No EMIB-T customer production for a ‘year or two’Intel's packaging footprint spans three continents. In Rio Rancho, New Mexico, Intel launched the Fab 9 facility in January 2024 as the first co-located high-volume advanced packaging site, adjacent to the existing Fab 11X and backed by a $3.5 billion investment. Intel later received $500 million in CHIPS Act funding earmarked for further modernization of Fab 9, Fab 11, and Fab 11X.In Asia, the Penang, Malaysia, advanced packaging complex is 99% complete, with first-phase operations beginning sometime this year. Intel has also outsourced EMIB production to Amkor’s Songdo K5 facility in South Korea — described as a “first-ever” outsourcing move by TrendForce — with additional Amkor sites planned in Portugal and Arizona.However, EMIB-T itself hasn’t yet shipped in any commercial product. Standard EMIB has been in volume since 2017 and is what every named Intel packaging customer is currently using, but the TSV-enhanced variant is only now due to roll out, with external customer designs expected in production "in the next year or two," says Mark Gardner.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 英特尔EMIB-T封装技术预计今年投产；台积电CoWoS产能紧张之际，EMIB-T正为AI加速器先进设计蓄力
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔首席财务官戴夫·津斯纳在上月摩根士丹利TMT会议上透露，英特尔代工服务（IFS）仅靠先进封装业务就“即将敲定几笔年均价值数十亿美元的交易”，其中下一代嵌入式桥接技术EMIB-T正推动客户兴趣增长，而台积电的CoWoS-L产能目前仍处于结构性供不应求状态。

这项名为EMIB-T的技术通过在桥接芯片中增加硅通孔（TSV），预计将于今年进入生产阶段，旨在解决标准EMIB技术无法应用于高功耗AI加速器的限制，包括满足HBM4级别的供电需求以及实现更大尺寸封装扩展。若这些年均“数十亿美元”的交易得以落实，将标志着英特尔代工业务的重大转折——该部门去年外部营收仅3.07亿美元，运营亏损却高达103亿美元。考虑到英特尔18A制程预计最早明年才能达到行业标准良率，先进封装成为其最快切入市场的路径，而EMIB-T正是将其转化为AI加速器营收的关键技术。

**技术原理与演进**
自2017年量产的标准EMIB技术将小型硅桥接芯片嵌入有机基板空腔中，实现相邻芯粒间的水平信号互连，避免了使用全硅中介层的成本和光罩尺寸限制。英特尔当时刻意在EMIB中完全避开硅通孔，以保持桥接芯片结构简单、成本低廉。但这也意味着电力必须通过有机基板绕开桥接芯片传输，路径长、电阻大，限制了传输至上方芯片的电流。这一限制对Sapphire Rapids和Ponte Vecchio等产品尚可接受，却无法满足HBM4级加速器的需求。

EMIB-T通过为桥接芯片添加硅通孔扭转了这一设计。该技术可实现垂直电力传输直接穿过桥接芯片，集成金属-绝缘体-金属电容以抑制噪声，并增加铜接地网格用于信号隔离。英特尔院士兼基板封装开发副总裁Rahul Manepalli博士在去年5月的电子元件技术会议上披露了EMIB-T完整规格：凸点间距45微米（路线图规划35/25微米），能效约0.25皮焦/比特，UCle-A接口每引脚数据传输速率达32Gb/s或更高。该技术支持HBM3、HBM3E、HBM4及未来HBM5堆栈，可扩展至120mm×180mm封装尺寸，容纳超过38个桥接芯片和12个以上光罩尺寸的芯粒。

**性能与成本优势**
去年圣诞节前，英特尔进一步展示了集成16个计算单元、24个HBM5堆栈、硅面积达10,296平方毫米的2.5D/3D概念封装方案，尺寸达12倍光罩，超越台积电计划中9.5倍光罩的CoWoS-L上限。对比显示，台积电CoWoS-L路线图目标为今年达5.5倍光罩、2027年达9.5倍光罩，而英特尔目标为2026年达8倍光罩、2028年达12倍或更大。

伯恩斯坦分析师估算存在显著成本差异：EMIB封装每芯片成本为数百美元低区间，而据Investing.com近期封装分析引用的数据，CoWoS在Rubin级加速器上的成本约900-1000美元。英特尔还宣称其桥接芯片晶圆利用率约90%，远高于大型中介层约60%的利用率。

**产能竞争格局**
台积电已将CoWoS月产能从2024年底的约3.5万片晶圆提升至2025年底的约8万片，并通过嘉义AP7和台南AP8新设施计划在今年底达到约13万片。Counterpoint Research预测，2026年全球先进封装产能可能同比增长约80%。然而，英伟达已锁定台积电2025-2026年超60%的CoWoS总产能，且每颗Blackwell GPU及即将推出的Rubin架构都需要CoWoS-L封装。2025年下半年有报道称，台积电CoWoS产能因AI需求紧绷，英特尔正承接那些无法获得CoWoS产能或寻求更短生产路径的公司的封装订单。

TrendForce去年12月初报告指出，CoWoS-L和CoWoS-S产能均已满载，谷歌因分配限制将2026年TPU目标产量削减100万颗。台积电CEO魏哲家在2025年第三季度财报电话会议中承认，AI相关的前后端产能仍然非常紧张，“约为AI需求的三分之一”。

**客户生态与迁移案例**
目前仅有少数客户被公开提及对EMIB-T感兴趣。去年11月，《工商时报》报道联发科正积极招募具有EMIB经验的工程师，并称英特尔正利用EMIB-T争取更多产能。亚马逊也被报道是另一个潜在客户，据称英特尔正与AWS就Trainium级定制AI处理器封装进行洽谈。

与此同时，标准EMIB已建立成熟的客户渠道。英伟达2025年9月对英特尔的50亿美元投资已确认将使用EMIB和Foveros技术，黄仁勋与陈立武在联合发布会上点名了这两项技术。Digitimes报道称约25%的Feynman GPU封装将通过英特尔完成。微软的Maia AI加速器是英特尔代工18A制程的旗舰客户，双方签署了150亿美元终身合约；而谷歌据悉正将EMIB设计纳入其2027年TPU v9。

英特尔代工封装副总裁Mark Gardner去年3月对《EE Times》表示：“我们现在已投入生产，甚至在去年就已开始生产那些原本为CoWoS设计、现改用EMIB或Foveros制造的产品。”这证实基于标准EMIB平台从台积电迁移设计的进程已在推进。

**产品应用路线**
Clearwater Forest（288核Xeon 6+ E核服务器处理器）采用第二代标准EMIB结合Foveros Direct 3D混合键合，在17芯粒封装中通过12个EMIB桥接。Diamond Rapids（最高192核Xeon 7 P核服务器处理器）也计划使用标准EMIB加Foveros。

而很可能首发搭载EMIB-T的英特尔产品是Jaguar Shores（已取消的Falcon Shores AI加速器后继者）。一款泄露的测试芯片显示其采用92.5mm×92.5mm封装，集成4个计算芯粒和8个HBM4接口——这恰恰是标准EMIB难以应对的用例，使Jaguar Shores成为该变体技术的自然首秀平台。值得注意的是，英特尔首款18A客户端处理器Panther Lake采用Foveros-S 2.5D而非任何EMIB变体。

**产能布局与时间表**
英特尔的封装产能遍布三大洲：2024年1月在新墨西哥州Rio Rancho启动的Fab 9是首个同址大规模先进封装基地，毗邻现有Fab 11X，获得35亿美元投资支持，随后又收到5亿美元CHIPS法案资金用于Fab 9/11/11X现代化改造。在亚洲，马来西亚槟城先进封装综合体已完成99%，第一阶段运营将于今年开始。英特尔还将EMIB生产外包给Amkor韩国Songdo K5工厂（TrendForce称此为“首次”外包），并计划在葡萄牙和亚利桑那州增设Amkor生产点。

然而，EMIB-T尚未在任何商用产品中出货。标准EMIB自2017年起量产，当前所有已公布的英特尔封装客户均在使用该版本，而增强TSV的变体技术刚刚开始推广。Mark Gardner表示，外部客户基于EMIB-T的设计预计“未来一两年内”投入生产。

items.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
items.description:
Today on Decoder, let’s talk about the looming AI monetization cliff, and whether some of the biggest companies in the space can become real, profitable businesses before they careen right off it.
My guest today is Hayden Field, who’s our senior AI reporter here at The Verge. She’s been keeping close tabs on both Anthropic and OpenAI, and how these two companies in particular tell us a whole lot about the AI industry in 2026. 
You’ve certainly heard a version of the monetization cliff story before. The biggest AI firms are built off the back of hundreds of billions in capital investment, and they’re linked to even greater amounts of forward-looking investment in data center build-out, chips, and other infrastructure spend. At some point, the profits have to materialize, or the bubble pops. Maybe AGI arrives, maybe the economy crashes, who knows. 
You’ve heard me ask some version of this question to scores of CEOs here on this show, and a majority of them have hinted toward the bubble popping — they think some companies will fail in spectacular fashion, some will succeed, and the opportunities, especially the money, are simply too big to ignore. We’re doing this, whether we want to or not — the market depends on it. 



Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here. 
So these last few weeks have felt like a very important inflection point, as both Anthropic and OpenAI have started to react to the reality of needing to go public — needing to make money, 
The catalyst for this change is AI agents, and products like Claude Code and Cowork, as well as the open-source OpenClaw and OpenAI’s Codex, have radically changed how these companies are thinking about their resources. And this is starting to affect how they behave — the products they support or suddenly kill, the restrictions they impose on customers, and the money they’re willing to burn toward their next big milestone. 
That’s because agents are valuable to customers right now, but agents also use far more compute. So the way people are using agents is burning tokens at a rate way faster than these companies anticipated, and that’s causing them to make hard decisions. 
We saw this most evidently last month when OpenAI abruptly killed its video-generation app Sora, ditching a $1 billion Disney licensing deal in the process. Why? It costs too much to run, and OpenAI needs the compute for Codex. We saw it again just last week, when Anthropic decided it would no longer let Claude users burn through compute resources using the OpenClaw agent framework through a standard subscription plan, instead forcing those users onto pay-as-you-go plans, which cost substantially more. 
As you’ll hear Hayden explain here, these are glimmers of a make-or-break moment for the AI industry, as both Anthropic and OpenAI barrel toward two of the biggest IPOs in history. And the pressure on these companies to make money has never been this intense. 
The projections these companies have made, which just this week were leaked to the Wall Street Journal, tell a story of mind-boggling growth, to the tune of hundreds of billions in revenue and profitability by the end of the decade. But the most important questions now are can the AI companies pull this off, and what compromises will they make to reach that goal and avoid crashing and burning? 
Okay: Verge senior policy reporter Hayden Field on the AI monetization cliff and the race to profitability. Here we go.


If you’d like to read more about what we discussed in this episode, check out these links:
The vibes are off at OpenAI | The Verge
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude | The Verge
Why OpenAI killed Sora | The Verge
OpenAI just bought TBPN | The Verge
National poll shows voters like AI less than ICE | The Verge
The spiraling cost of making AI | WSJ
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo taking leave amid exec shake-up | Wired
OpenAI raises another $122B at $850B valuation | The Verge
Questions or comments about this episode? Hit us up at decoder@theverge.com. We really do read every email!

items.author: Nilay Patel
items.titleZh: 现在AI行业的利润追逐战关乎存亡。
items.descriptionZh:
近期，AI行业正面临迫在眉睫的“盈利悬崖”——尽管顶级AI公司已获得数千亿美元资本投入，并承诺对数据中心、芯片等基础设施进行更大规模投资，但盈利压力已到达临界点。若无法实现可持续的商业模式，行业泡沫可能破裂。在这一背景下，Anthropic与OpenAI两家代表性企业的动向尤为关键，它们不仅预示着2026年AI产业的走向，更映射出整个行业在规模化盈利前的挣扎与抉择。

**核心技术动因：AI代理引发资源消耗危机**  
引发当前局面的直接技术因素是**AI代理（AI Agents）**的普及。例如Anthropic的Claude Code、Cowork，开源框架OpenClaw，以及OpenAI的Codex等产品，正在改变企业分配计算资源的方式。这些代理能够执行复杂多步任务，但同时也消耗远超预期的计算资源（token消耗速度远超公司预测），导致运营成本急剧上升。这迫使企业重新评估产品策略：支持高价值代理服务的同时，不得不削减或关闭其他高成本项目。

**战略调整案例：成本控制下的产品收缩**  
- **OpenAI关闭Sora视频生成应用**：尽管已与迪士尼达成10亿美元授权协议，但因Sora运行成本过高，OpenAI选择终止该项目，将计算资源集中投向Codex等代理相关产品。  
- **Anthropic限制OpenClaw访问**：近期Anthropic禁止标准订阅用户通过Claude使用OpenClaw框架，强制将其迁移至按量计费模式，显著提高使用门槛以控制资源消耗。

**财务压力与上市倒计时**  
两家公司均处于筹备历史上规模最大IPO的关键阶段，盈利压力空前。据《华尔街日报》泄露的财务预测，它们预计到2030年将实现数千亿美元营收与盈利。然而，实现该目标需要平衡两个矛盾：一方面必须扩大代理等高价值服务，另一方面需严格控制爆炸式增长的计算成本。当前举措——如收缩业务、调整订阅策略——正是其应对“盈利悬崖”的早期信号。

**行业影响与未来挑战**  
这一转折点揭示出AI行业普遍困境：  
1. **资本依赖与盈利路径的冲突**：巨额前期投资需通过规模化商用兑现，但现有技术栈（尤其是大模型推理）的成本结构尚未优化至可盈利水平。  
2. **客户行为改变商业模型**：用户对代理的高频使用正在打破企业原有的资源规划模型，迫使企业重新设计计费体系与服务边界。  
3. **技术决策与商业目标的捆绑**：公司不再单纯追求技术突破，而是依据资源消耗与盈利潜力决定产品存续，技术路线日益服从于财务指标。

**应用场景的重塑**  
短期内，面向企业的代理服务（如代码生成、复杂流程自动化）将成为营收焦点，因其能直接提升客户生产力并支持更高定价。而面向消费者的轻量级应用（如创意生成工具）可能因成本受限而收缩。同时，行业或将出现更多“分层服务”模式：基础功能维持订阅制，高阶代理服务则全面转向按量计费。

当前Anthropic与OpenAI的抉择仅是开端。随着更多AI公司逼近盈利临界点，行业将进入残酷的筛选阶段——唯有在技术价值、成本控制与商业化节奏间找到平衡的企业，才可能跨越“盈利悬崖”，真正成长为可持续的商业模式。否则，资本泡沫破裂与技术收缩的连锁反应将不可避免。

items.title: Intel and SambaNova team up on heterogenous AI inference platform — different hardware performs different workloads
items.description: Intel and SambaNova announce heterogeneous inference platform that can take advantage of Intel Xeon 6 CPUs, SambaNova SN50 RDUs, and Nvidia GPUs.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel and SambaNova on Wednesday announced their joint production-ready heterogeneous inference architecture that relies on AI accelerators or GPUs for prefill, SambaNova reconfigurable dataflow units (RDUs) SN50 for decode, and Xeon 6 processors for agentic tools and system orchestration. The platform is designed to address as broad a set of workloads as possible to siphon some of the market share away from Nvidia and other emerging players.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe heterogeneous inference platform by Intel and SambaNova separates inference into distinct stages handled by different silicon: It uses AI GPUs or AI accelerators for ingesting long prompts and building key-value caches; SambaNova's SN50 RDU for decoding and generating tokens; and Xeon 6 processors for running agent-related operations (e.g., compiling and executing code and validating outputs) as well as coordinating and distributing workloads across hardware. Splitting prefill, decode, and token generation stages is similar to Nvidia's approach to its Rubin platform, which is based on the Rubin CPX and heavy-duty Rubin GPU with HBM4 memory — with the obvious difference that the Rubin CPX is not coming to market. But, more importantly for Intel, the new platform will rely on its Xeon 6 processors — not on competing offerings. (Image credit: SambaNova)The solution is scheduled to be available in the second half of 2026 to enterprises, cloud operators, and sovereign AI programs seeking scalable inference platforms in general and coding agents, and other agentic workloads in particular, completely in-house.According to SambaNova's internal data, Xeon 6 achieves over 50% faster LLVM compilation compared to Arm-based server CPUs, and delivers up to 70% higher performance in vector database workloads, relative to competing x86 processors — namely, AMD EPYC. These gains are intended to shorten end-to-end development cycles for coding agents and similar applications, the two companies claim.Perhaps the biggest advantage of the joint production-ready heterogeneous inference architecture is that SambaNova SN50 and Xeon-based servers are drop-in compatible with data centers that can handle 30kW — which is the vast majority of enterprise data centers. "The data center software ecosystem is built on x86, and it runs on Xeon — providing a mature, proven foundation that developers, enterprises, and cloud providers rely on at scale," said Kevork Kechichian, Executive Vice President and General Manager of the Data Center Group (DCG) at Intel Corporation. "Workloads of the future will require a heterogeneous mix of computing, and this collaboration with SambaNova delivers a cost‑efficient, high‑performance inference architecture designed to meet customer needs at scale — powered by Xeon 6."Article continues below You may like Sambanova introduces new AI accelerator, partners with Intel to deploy Xeon CPUs for inferencing and agentic workloads Intel's roadmap adds mysterious 'hybrid' AI processor featuring x86 CPUs, dedicated AI accelerator, and programmable IP Intel Xeon 6 selected as host CPU for Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 英特尔携手SambaNova共推异构AI推理平台——差异化硬件运行不同工作负载
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔与AI加速器公司SambaNova于本周三联合宣布，推出其生产就绪的异构推理架构。该平台旨在通过结合不同硬件的优势，处理尽可能广泛的工作负载，以从英伟达及其他新兴竞争者手中争夺市场份额。

**背景与核心架构**
该异构推理平台的核心思想是将AI推理过程，特别是大语言模型（LLM）的推理，分解为不同阶段，并由专用硬件处理，以实现整体效率最大化。具体分工如下：
1.  **预填充（Prefill）阶段**：由AI GPU或AI加速器负责。此阶段处理长提示输入，并构建关键的键值（KV）缓存，为后续生成做好准备。
2.  **解码（Decode）与令牌生成阶段**：由SambaNova的**SN50可重构数据流单元（RDU）** 负责。这是生成文本输出的核心阶段。
3.  **智能体工具与系统协调阶段**：由英特尔的**至强6（Xeon 6）处理器**负责。它运行与AI智能体相关的操作（如编译和执行代码、验证输出），并负责在整套硬件间协调和分发工作负载。

这种将预填充、解码与生成分离的思路，与英伟达基于Rubin CPX和Rubin GPU的Rubin平台策略相似。然而，关键区别在于，英特尔- SambaNova方案的核心协调与智能体处理单元是实际即将上市的至强6处理器，而非尚未面市的Rubin CPX。

**性能优势**
根据SambaNova的内部数据，至强6处理器在特定工作负载上展现出显著优势：
*   与基于Arm架构的服务器CPU相比，在LLVM编译任务上快**50%以上**。
*   与竞争x86处理器（即AMD EPYC）相比，在向量数据库工作负载上性能提升高达**70%**。
这些性能提升旨在缩短编码智能体（Coding Agent）等应用的端到端开发周期。

**技术影响与部署前景**
该解决方案计划于**2026年下半年**向企业、云运营商以及寻求构建主权AI的机构提供，特别适合那些需要可扩展推理平台，并专注于编码智能体和其他智能体工作负载的客户。

其一大突出优势在于**部署便利性**：SambaNova SN50加速卡与基于至强处理器的服务器，能够“即插即用”地兼容绝大多数现有企业数据中心（支持30kW功率规格），无需大规模改造基础设施。英特尔数据中心事业部总经理Kevork Kechichian指出，数据中心软件生态系统本就建立在x86架构并以至强为基础，这为开发者与企业提供了一个成熟、可靠的规模化部署基石。

**总结**
英特尔与SambaNova的合作，标志着面向复杂AI工作负载（尤其是智能体应用）的异构计算架构正加速迈向成熟商用。通过将GPU/加速器、专用AI计算单元（RDU）和通用CPU进行任务级优化整合，该平台试图在性能、能效和现有生态兼容性之间取得平衡，为挑战英伟达在AI推理领域的统治地位提供了一条差异化路径。至强6处理器被定位为未来异构计算混合负载中的系统“大脑”与协调中心，确保了英特尔在AI数据中心的核心地位。

history.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
history.description:
Google's latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the AI-generated model, manually adjust sliders on it, or input different values to change the simulation in real-time.
When trying out the feature for myself, I asked Gemini to make a simulation of the Moon orbiting the Earth, and it created a 3D model with a few different ways to interact with it. Along with a slider to adjust the speed of the Moon's orbit, there's also a toggle to hide the line representing its orbital path and a button to pause the  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Google’s latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the AI-generated model, manually adjust sliders on it, or input different values to change the simulation in real-time.When trying out the feature for myself, I asked Gemini to make a simulation of the Moon orbiting the Earth, and it created a 3D model with a few different ways to interact with it. Along with a slider to adjust the speed of the Moon’s orbit, there’s also a toggle to hide the line representing its orbital path and a button to pause the simulation. You can also zoom in on and rotate the 3D model.There are a few different ways to interact with the simulation. Screenshot: The VergeThis update comes just weeks after Anthropic gave Claude the ability to automatically respond with charts, diagrams, and other interactive visuals, while OpenAI added a feature to ChatGPT that allows it to generate visualizations of math and science concepts. Previously, Gemini could only generate interactive images in response to prompts.All Gemini app users can access this feature by selecting the “Pro” model in the prompt bar. From there, ask Gemini something like, “show me a double pendulum,” or “help me visualize the Doppler effect,” and then select the “Show me the visualization” button beneath Gemini’s response.
history.author: Emma Roth
history.titleZh: 谷歌的Gemini人工智能可以通过3D模型和模拟来回答问题
history.descriptionZh:
谷歌近期为Gemini对话机器人推出重要升级，使其能够根据用户问题生成交互式3D模型与动态模拟。这一功能突破意味着用户可通过旋转AI生成的模型、手动调节滑块控件或输入不同数值，实现对模拟场景的实时动态调整。

在技术演示中，用户向Gemini提出“模拟月球绕地球运行”的请求后，系统生成的可交互3D模型提供了多重控制维度：除了调节月球公转速度的滑块外，还设有轨道显示开关、模拟暂停按钮，并支持对三维模型的自由缩放与视角旋转。这种交互设计使得抽象科学概念得以直观呈现。

此次升级正值AI视觉化功能竞争白热化阶段——数周前Anthropic为Claude添加了自动生成图表与交互视图的能力，OpenAI也为ChatGPT部署了数学与科学概念可视化功能。相比之下，此前Gemini仅支持生成静态交互图像，新版3D模拟功能标志着其多模态生成能力的显著跃升。

在技术架构层面，该功能依托Gemini Pro模型实现，用户只需在输入栏选择“Pro”模式，提出如“展示双摆系统”或“帮我可视化多普勒效应”等请求，待系统回复后点击“显示可视化”按钮即可激活交互模拟。这一升级不仅深化了教育、科研领域的沉浸式演示场景，也为工程建模、数据可视化等专业应用开辟了新路径。目前所有Gemini应用用户均可通过标准界面访问此功能。

history.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
history.description: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]
history.content: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual environments to real-world deployment faster than ever. With NVIDIA platforms for simulation, synthetic data and AI-powered robot learning, developers now have the tools to build machines that can perceive, reason and act in complex environments. Check back here all week for coverage on the latest NVIDIA physical AI technologies. Smarter Palletizing With AI-Driven Reasoning 🔗 In warehouse environments, palletizing robots typically follow fixed rules — handling boxes the same way regardless of contents, condition or fragility. A project developed by Doosan Robotics introduces a more adaptive approach using NVIDIA Cosmos Reason. By analyzing a single camera image, the system can infer box contents, detect damage and adjust how each item is handled — such as placement, speed and grip — based on estimated weight and fragility. This reduces common issues like incorrectly stacking damaged or fragile goods. To build robots that understand the physical world before they ever deploy in it, robotics researchers and developers are building policy models powered by NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models (WFMs). Toyota Research Institute customizes Cosmos WFMs for their own world model to achieve state-of-the-art results across dynamic view synthesis, state-of-the-art teleoperation data augmentation and navigation world models. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/nvidia_cosmos_accelerates_ai_training_for_robotics.mp4 Mimic robotics takes a different angle with mimic-video, a video-action model that pairs a pretrained internet-scale video model with a flow-matching action decoder, replacing the static image-language backbones of traditional VLAs with video-learned physical dynamics — achieving 10x better sample efficiency and 2x faster convergence on real-world manipulation tasks. Together, both teams demonstrate a fundamental shift: robots trained on world models that capture physics and causality need dramatically less real-world data to perform reliably in conditions they’ve never seen. Open, Intelligent Robotics on NVIDIA Jetson: Community Innovations Powering the Next Wave of Physical AI 🔗 This National Robotics Week, OpenClaw running on the NVIDIA Jetson platform showcases how quickly open source innovation is evolving into real-world, intelligent robotics. From practical applications to innovative projects, the robotics community is building what’s next — and fast. Developers are pushing the boundaries of autonomy — including hardware-in-the-loop testing powered by Jetson Thor, evaluating camera streams from NVIDIA Isaac Sim and even building systems that can generate their own code to complete tasks. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/oss-on-jetson-models-nrw.mp4 In addition, OpenClaw now running entirely locally on NVIDIA Jetson Thor — powered by optimized NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the vLLM open inference library — marks a major leap toward private, low-latency edge AI for robotics. And innovations like the NVIDIA NemoClaw stack on Jetson are expanding what’s possible at the intersection of open source and high-performance robotics platforms. Training and Refining Movement in Simulation 🔗 Gennady Plyushchev, a robotics creator known as Skyentific, is documenting the process of building a walking bipedal robot, from simulation and design to real-world deployment — showcasing a simulation-first approach to robot development. By using NVIDIA Isaac based simulation workflows alongside NVIDIA Jetson for on-device AI and control, the project demonstrates how developers can rapidly iterate in virtual environments before deploying to physical systems. The result highlights a broader shift in robotics: using AI, simulation and edge computing to accelerate development and bring increasingly capable humanoid robots to life. University of Maryland Researchers Develop Robots for Complex Household Tasks 🔗 To bring robots into everyday life, researchers at the University of Maryland, recipients of a grant from the NVIDIA Academic Grant Program, are developing AI-powered humanoid systems capable of performing complex household tasks with greater autonomy. The project centers on building robot foundation models that unify perception, planning and control. Using the NVIDIA Isaac open robotics development platform, researchers can create photorealistic, high-fidelity virtual home environments populated with diverse objects and layouts, allowing robots to practice millions of task variations and safely test rare or complex scenarios. NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs for training large models and NVIDIA Jetson AGX Thor developer kits for efficient deployment on physical robots help bridge the gap between research and real-world applications. By combining advancements in generative AI, sequential decision-making and scalable computing, the work represents a key step toward general-purpose robots that can support people in homes, healthcare settings and beyond. Announcing the MassRobotics Fellowship 🔗 The second cohort of the Amazon Web Services (AWS) MassRobotics fellowship comprises startups being recognized for compelling industrial use cases harnessing robotics and computer vision. They will receive access to technical resources and AWS cloud credits. The cohort includes NVIDIA Inception members Burro, Config Intelligence, Deltia, Haply Robotics, Luminous Robotics, Roboto AI, Telexistence, Terra Robotics and WiRobotics, each developing technologies spanning humanoid robotics, industrial automation, haptics and agricultural systems. Burro creates autonomous agricultural robots for tasks like grape harvesting and crop scouting. Config Intelligence builds data infrastructure for general-purpose bimanual robotics to enable reliable two-handed tasks in real-world settings. Deltia provides AI-driven manufacturing intelligence that optimizes assembly lines using computer vision and analytics. Haply Robotics designs haptic control devices that serve as “steering wheels” for physical AI systems across industries. Luminous Robotics deploys AI-powered robotic systems for fast, low-cost solar-panel installation and maintenance. Roboto AI offers a data-analytics platform that accelerates robot development by managing and analyzing robotics data. Telexistence develops AI-powered humanoid robots and remote-controlled systems for retail and logistics. Terra Robotics develops laser-weeding agricultural robots to automate sustainable farming. WiRobotics creates wearable walking-assist and humanoid robots to enhance mobility and physical interaction, using training data from assisted products to train its humanoids. Accelerating How Utility-Scale Solar Projects Are Built in the Field 🔗 Maximo, a solar robotics business incubated within The AES Corporation, recently completed a 100-megawatt solar installation using its robot fleet. Developed with NVIDIA accelerated computing, NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and the NVIDIA Isaac Sim framework, Maximo demonstrated that autonomous installations can operate reliably for utility-scale projects. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/maximo-nrw.mp4 The solution improves installation speed, safety and consistency, helping close the gap between rising demand for faster time to power and construction capacity. As solar expansion faces ongoing labor constraints and rising demand, AI-driven field robotics systems like Maximo are helping accelerate infrastructure buildout, reduce costs and redefine how energy projects are delivered. Aigen Advances Sustainable Farming With Agricultural Robotics 🔗 To help regenerate the Earth, Aigen’s solar-powered autonomous robots are breaking farmers’ dependency on chemicals through precision weed control powered by vision AI. The NVIDIA Inception startup is building a new kind of farming system that’s powered by clean energy and continuously enriched by data. Aigen’s fleet of solar-driven rovers uses advanced computer vision to identify and remove weeds, dramatically reducing the need for herbicides. Farming has no standard environment. Every field is different — different crops, different soil, different equipment, weeds, growth stages and geographies. That fragmentation makes real-world data collection slow, expensive and inconsistent. By post-training NVIDIA Cosmos open world foundation models on their specialized data and harnessing NVIDIA Isaac Sim pipelines, Aigen is building the system that generalizes for millions of agriculture scenarios. On the ground, each rover runs inference using an NVIDIA Jetson Orin edge AI module to distinguish crops from weeds in real time. https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Aigen_Element_Weeding_1.mp4 Using these rovers, farmers can grow crops more sustainably and profitably, using regenerative practices that heal the land and foster ecological balance.
history.author: NVIDIA Writers
history.titleZh: 国家机器人周：最新实体AI研究、突破与资源速览
history.descriptionZh:
适逢美国国家机器人周，英伟达重点展示了将人工智能带入物理世界的突破性进展，以及正在变革农业、制造业、能源等各行业的机器人浪潮。机器人学习、仿真和基础模型领域的进步正在加速发展，使机器人能够比以往更快地从虚拟环境训练转向现实世界部署。借助英伟达的仿真、合成数据和AI驱动的机器人学习平台，开发者现在拥有了构建能在复杂环境中感知、推理和行动的机器所需的工具。

在仓库环境中，传统的堆垛机器人通常遵循固定规则，无论箱内物品、状况或易碎性如何，都以相同方式处理。斗山机器人公司开发的一个项目引入了更自适应的方法，它利用英伟达Cosmos Reason推理技术。该系统通过分析单张相机图像，能够推断箱内物品、检测损坏情况，并根据估计的重量和易碎性调整对每个物品的处理方式，如放置位置、速度和抓握力度。这减少了错误堆叠损坏或易碎物品等常见问题。

为了打造在部署前就能理解物理世界的机器人，研究人员和开发者正在构建由英伟达Cosmos世界基础模型驱动的策略模型。丰田研究院为其自己的世界模型定制了Cosmos WFM，在动态视图合成、最先进的遥操作数据增强和导航世界模型方面取得了顶尖成果。

模仿机器人技术则采用了不同的角度，其“模仿-视频”模型将预训练的互联网规模视频模型与流匹配动作解码器相结合，用视频学习到的物理动力学取代了传统视觉语言动作模型中静态的图像-语言骨干网络。该模型在现实世界操作任务上实现了10倍的样本效率提升和2倍的收敛速度提升。这些团队共同展示了一个根本性的转变：在捕捉物理和因果关系的世界模型上训练的机器人，在从未见过的环境中可靠运行时，所需真实世界数据量大幅减少。

在本届机器人周，运行在英伟达Jetson平台上的OpenClaw项目展示了开源创新正如何快速演变为现实世界的智能机器人。从实际应用到创新项目，机器人社区正在快速构建未来。开发者们正在突破自主性的边界，包括利用Jetson Thor进行硬件在环测试、评估来自英伟达Isaac Sim的相机流，甚至构建能够自主生成代码以完成任务。

此外，OpenClaw现已完全在英伟达Jetson Thor上本地运行，由优化的英伟达Nemotron开放模型和vLLM开放推理库提供支持，这标志着机器人向私有化、低延迟的边缘AI迈出了一大步。像英伟达NemoClaw堆栈这样的创新，正在扩展开源与高性能机器人平台交叉领域的可能性。

名为Skyentific的机器人创客正在记录构建双足行走机器人的全过程，从仿真设计到现实世界部署，展示了机器人开发的“仿真优先”方法。通过使用基于英伟达Isaac的仿真工作流以及用于设备端AI和控制的Jetson平台，该项目展示了开发者如何能在部署到物理系统之前在虚拟环境中快速迭代。这突显了机器人领域一个更广泛的转变：利用AI、仿真和边缘计算加速开发，让能力日益强大的人形机器人成为现实。

为了让机器人走进日常生活，马里兰大学的研究人员正在开发能够以更高自主性执行复杂家庭任务的AI驱动人形系统。该项目核心是构建统一感知、规划与控制功能的机器人基础模型。利用英伟达Isaac开放机器人开发平台，研究人员可以创建包含多样化物体和布局的逼真高保真虚拟家庭环境，让机器人练习数百万种任务变体，并安全测试罕见或复杂场景。用于训练大模型的英伟达RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU和用于在物理机器人上高效部署的Jetson AGX Thor开发套件，帮助弥合了研究与现实应用之间的鸿沟。这项结合了生成式AI、序列决策和可扩展计算进步的工作，是迈向能够在家庭、医疗等场景支持人类的通用机器人的关键一步。

同时，由亚马逊云科技支持的第二期MassRobotics fellowship项目已宣布，入选的初创公司因其利用机器人和计算机视觉的引人注目的工业用例而受到认可。他们将获得技术资源和AWS云积分支持。这些公司包括英伟达初创加速计划成员Burro、Config Intelligence、Deltia、Haply Robotics、Luminous Robotics、Roboto AI、Telexistence、Terra Robotics和WiRobotics，其技术涵盖人形机器人、工业自动化、触觉反馈和农业系统等多个领域。

在能源领域，从AES公司内部孵化的太阳能机器人企业Maximo，近期使用其机器人车队完成了一个100兆瓦的太阳能安装项目。该方案利用英伟达加速计算、Omniverse库和Isaac Sim框架开发，证明了自主安装系统能够可靠地用于公用事业规模的项目。该解决方案提高了安装速度、安全性和一致性，有助于缩小日益增长的对快速通电的需求与施工能力之间的差距。随着太阳能扩张面临持续的劳动力限制和不断增长的需求，像Maximo这样的AI驱动现场机器人系统正在帮助加速基础设施建设，降低成本，并重新定义能源项目的交付方式。

为了帮助地球再生，初创公司Aigen的太阳能自主机器人通过视觉AI驱动的精准除草，正在打破农民对化学品的依赖。这家英伟达初创加速计划成员正在构建一种由清洁能源驱动、并持续被数据丰富的新型农业系统。Aigen的太阳能驱动漫游车车队使用先进的计算机视觉来识别和清除杂草，大幅减少了对除草剂的需求。农业没有标准环境，每个田地都不同。通过在其专业数据上对英伟达Cosmos开放世界基础模型进行后训练，并利用英伟达Isaac Sim管线，Aigen正在构建能够泛化适用于数百万农业场景的系统。在地面上，每台漫游车都使用英伟达Jetson Orin边缘AI模块进行实时推理，以区分作物和杂草。使用这些漫游车，农民可以采用再生农业实践，以更可持续和有利可图的方式种植作物，修复土地并促进生态平衡。

history.title: Alleged images of the long-awaited Nvidia N1/N1X SoC surface on laptop motherboard — board features 128 GB of LPDDR5X memory alongside 8+6+2 phase VRM
history.description: Nvidia's N1 SoC leaks yet again, but this time in the flesh, with potentially a laptop motherboard featuring the long-rumored chip. It was spotted on sale on a Chinese marketplace for roughly $1,400. The pictures show the PCB rocking 128 GB of LPDDR5X-8533 RAM, robust power delivery, and a decent I/O selection.

Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia's N1/N1X SoC has been driving the rumor mill for years at this point, but we've recently begun to see some leaks that point toward the chips finally releasing this year. The latest piece of evidence comes from Goofish, a reselling platform in China, where someone has just listed a laptop motherboard allegedly featuring the N1 SoC in all its glory. It's priced at around $1,400 (9999 RMB), but offers have been closed.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe pictures show a small motherboard that could be from a 13-inch tablet (similar to ROG Flow Z13) or, more likely, a 14-inch laptop, as it houses a large cutout for a fan. The N1 SoC can be seen on the right side; it's the biggest part on the board, flanked by eight memory chips and what looks like a robust 8+6+2 phase VRM setup. That beefy power delivery suggests that the N1 will feature a significant power appetite.Upon closer inspection, the eight memory ICs are SK hynix H58G78CK8B modules, totaling up to 128 GB of LPDDR5X RAM running at 8,533 MT/s. For context, Strix Halo tops out at 8,000 MT/s while Panther Lake can go up to 9,600 MT/s in flagship configurations. Apple's M5 series also supports 9,600 MT/s across the board, and a report from WSJ previously said N1 is designed to directly compete with Apple Silicon.Article continues below You may like Nvidia's N1/N1X SoCs tipped for release in first half of 2026, debuting on Dell & Lenovo laptops Nvidia's Arm-based N1X-equipped gaming laptops are reportedly set to debut this quarter, with N2 series chips planned for 2027 Nvidia's elusive N1X SoC leaks out again (Image credit: Goofish)Anyhow, this motherboard also has plenty of ports — HDMI, USB Type-A, USB-C, and a 3.5mm headphone jack on one side, but none on the other. We can see 2x M.2 slots for 2240-sized SSDs, and there's a shielded communication antenna in the bottom-right corner for Bluetooth and Wi-Fi. It's a fairly densely packed PCB that appears more like a close-to-final, retail unit than an early engineering sample.As for the N1 itself, we know that the GB10 Superchip inside DGX Spark is based on N1 silicon, as confirmed by CEO Jensen Huang himself. It reportedly features a 20-core Arm-based CPU (10 cores per cluster) and an RTX 5070-level GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores. It's being developed in conjunction with MediaTek, which handles the CPU side, while Nvidia is, of course, in charge of the graphics and software. (Image credit: Goofish)The chip is so significant because after years of failed efforts, Nvidia has the chance to reinvigorate Microsoft's Windows-on-Arm initiative with silicon that can actually rival legacy options. Qualcomm's GPU drivers have held back the Snapdragon X Elite family, so Nvidia has the perfect gap to fill here. Once launched, this will be the Green Team's first consumer CPU since the Tegra X1 in the Shield TV back in 2015.All signs are now pointing toward a Computex 2026 reveal for the N1/N1X lineup after it missed GTC last month. A DigiTimes report from earlier this year claimed that Nvidia is targeting Q1 2026 for launch with more variants to follow in the second quarter. In January, we even saw a leaked shipping manifest for a Dell XPS laptop featuring an N1 engineering sample, suggesting that Dell is at least testing/tested the waters.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe Goofish listing claims that whatever device this motherboard is from is coming in the second half of the year, too, bringing "consumer-grade AI PCs" to the public. The board is also mentioned as an "Nvidia N1 AI book engineering sample" and that it "should be used for tablet computers." The text is translated, but the general sentiment is still carried over: N1 is intended for both conventional laptops and hybrid 2-in-1 devices. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 备受期待的英伟达N1/N1X SoC芯片组疑似曝光——主板搭载128GB LPDDR5X内存，并配备8+6+2相供电模块。
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达（NVIDIA）传闻已久的N1/N1X系统级芯片（SoC）近期再次出现实质性泄露，种种迹象表明其可能于2026年正式发布，旨在为消费级AI PC市场带来变革。

**背景与泄露来源**
多年来，关于英伟达N1/N1X SoC的传闻持续不断。最新证据来自中国二手平台“闲鱼”，有卖家列出了据称搭载N1 SoC的笔记本电脑主板，标价约1400美元（9999元人民币），但目前已关闭交易。此次泄露的主板照片提供了关于这颗芯片及其设计目标的重要线索。

**主板设计与硬件配置**
泄露照片显示，这是一块尺寸较小的主板，可能用于13英寸平板（类似ROG Flow Z13）或更可能用于14英寸笔记本电脑，因为它预留了大尺寸风扇开孔。主板的右侧可见N1 SoC芯片，它是板上最大的组件，两侧环绕着八颗内存芯片，并配备了看似非常扎实的8+6+2相VRM供电模组，这暗示了N1芯片将拥有较高的功耗需求。

具体硬件细节包括：
- **内存**：八颗SK海力士H58G78CK8B内存模块，总计提供高达128GB的LPDDR5X内存，运行速度为8533 MT/s。作为对比，AMD的Strix Halo最高支持8000 MT/s，英特尔下一代Panther Lake旗舰配置可达9600 MT/s，而苹果M5系列也全线支持9600 MT/s。《华尔街日报》此前曾报道，N1的设计目标正是直接与苹果自研芯片（Apple Silicon）竞争。
- **扩展与接口**：主板配备了HDMI、USB Type-A、USB-C和3.5mm耳机插孔（位于一侧），并提供了两个支持2240尺寸的M.2 SSD插槽。右下角还有一个带屏蔽的通信天线区域，用于蓝牙和Wi-Fi。其PCB布局密集，看起来更接近最终零售版本，而非早期工程样品。

**芯片规格与核心技术**
根据已知信息，用于DGX Spark超级计算机的GB10超级芯片正是基于N1硅片，这一点已得到英伟达CEO黄仁勋的证实。据报道，N1 SoC集成了：
- **CPU**：基于Arm架构的20核CPU（采用每集群10核心的设计）。
- **GPU**：性能相当于RTX 5070级别的GPU，拥有6144个CUDA核心。
该芯片由英伟达与联发科（MediaTek）联合开发，其中联发科负责CPU部分，而英伟达则主导图形处理单元和软件生态。

**市场意义与竞争定位**
这款芯片之所以备受关注，是因为在经过多年尝试后，英伟达有望凭借一颗真正能与传统x86选项匹敌的芯片，重振微软的“Windows-on-Arm”计划。此前，高通（Qualcomm）Snapdragon X Elite系列因GPU驱动问题受到制约，这为英伟达填补市场空白提供了绝佳机会。一旦发布，这将是英伟达自2015年Shield TV搭载Tegra X1以来，再次推出面向消费级的CPU产品。

**发布时间与合作伙伴**
目前所有线索均指向英伟达将在2026年台北国际电脑展（Computex 2026）上正式发布N1/N1X系列产品线，此前它已错过上个月的GTC大会。今年早些时候的DigiTimes报告声称，英伟达目标在2026年第一季度首发，更多变体将在第二季度跟进。一月份曾有一份泄露的货运清单显示，戴尔（Dell）一款XPS笔记本电脑搭载了N1工程样品，这表明戴尔至少已在对其进行测试。

**应用场景**
闲鱼列表中的描述提到，搭载该主板的设备将于今年下半年面世，旨在向公众推出“消费级AI PC”。该主板被描述为“Nvidia N1 AI book工程样品”，并提及“应用于平板电脑”。虽然文字经过翻译，但核心信息明确：N1芯片的目标设备既包括传统笔记本电脑，也涵盖混合形态的二合一设备。

综上所述，英伟达N1 SoC的泄露揭示了其在高端移动计算市场的雄心，凭借强大的Arm CPU、高性能GPU以及对高速内存的支持，意图在AI PC时代与苹果、高通和传统x86阵营展开直接竞争。其最终表现将取决于实际的能效、软件优化及合作伙伴的终端产品。

history.title: Intel and Google announce multi-year chip deal — Google will deploy Intel Xeon with custom IPUs for next-gen AI, cloud infrastructure
history.description: Although Google now has its own Arm-based Axion CPUs, Intel's Xeon processors with custom IPUs will continue to be used for AI and other demanding workloads in Google's data centers for years.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel and Google on Thursday announced a multi-year collaboration under which Google will continue deploying Intel Xeon platforms for its next generation of AI and cloud infrastructure. These platforms will rely not only on Intel's upcoming Xeon CPUs, but also on custom infrastructure processing units (IPUs) co-designed by Intel and Google. The announcement comes amid the accelerating adoption of custom Arm-based processors for AI workloads."Scaling AI requires more than accelerators - it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand," said Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Intel.Google currently employs Intel Xeon 5 and Intel Xeon 6 processors for a variety of workloads, including large-scale AI training coordination, latency-sensitive inference, and general-purpose computing. For example, Intel's latest Xeon CPUs power C4 and N4 instances. Although Google's custom Armv9-based Axion processors provide the cloud giant more control and efficiency at lower cost, many workloads that are run in Google's data centers need to either be backwards compatible with x86 or just need maximum single-thread performance offered by Intel Xeon CPUs. This is something that is expected to continue for years to come, which is why the two companies inked the deal.Article continues below You may like Report claims Arm chips will power 90% of AI servers based on custom processors in 2029 Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging Intel Xeon 6 selected as host CPU for Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems In a bid to make Intel Xeon platforms more efficient and suitable for its hyperscale data centers, Google will also co-develop custom IPUs together with Intel to offload networking, storage, and security functions from host CPUs. Ultimately, Intel Xeon platforms will combine x86 architecture with high single-thread performance and custom-built infrastructure processing, which will make them more competitive in Google's highly customized environments."CPUs and infrastructure acceleration remain a cornerstone of AI systems — from training orchestration to inference and deployment," said Amin Vahdat, SVP & Chief Technologist, AI Infrastructure, Google.The announcement comes at a time when hyperscalers and AI platform developers are accelerating the adoption of their own custom CPUs based on the Arm instruction set architecture. Just a week ago, Counterpoint Research released a note claiming that 90% of AI servers running custom-silicon processors will rely on the Arm ISA, leaving x86 and RISC-V about 10%. The announcement by Intel and Google clearly states that Xeon CPUs with custom IPUs will continue to be used for AI and other demanding workloads for years to come, which is something to be expected anyway.Intel's Xeon processors have powered cloud infrastructure since its inception in the 2000s, and Google's own servers before that, so x86 in general and Xeon in particular will not leave Google's data center premises any time soon. Nonetheless, the announcement clearly reemphasizes the relevance of Intel's Xeon CPUs, and when such a message comes from Google — which has been deploying special-purpose custom accelerators for years across virtually all of its services — it gets amplified significantly.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors"Intel has been a trusted partner for nearly two decades, and their Xeon roadmap gives us confidence that we can continue to meet the growing performance and efficiency demands of our workloads," Vahdat added.
