history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 英特尔与谷歌达成多年芯片协议——将为下一代AI和云端设施部署配备定制IPU的英特尔至强处理器
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔与谷歌于近期宣布达成一项多年期合作，谷歌将在其下一代人工智能与云基础设施中继续部署英特尔至强平台。该平台不仅将搭载英特尔即将推出的至强中央处理器，还将采用双方共同设计的定制基础设施处理器（IPU）。这一合作背景正值行业加速采用基于Arm架构的定制处理器处理人工智能负载的趋势。

英特尔首席执行官Pat Gelsinger与谷歌人工智能基础设施高级副总裁兼首席技术官Amin Vahdat均强调，扩展人工智能不仅需要加速器，更需要平衡的系统。CPU与IPU对于满足现代人工智能工作负载对性能、效率与灵活性的需求至关重要。谷歌目前已在多种工作负载中使用英特尔至强5和至强6处理器，包括大规模人工智能训练协调、延迟敏感型推理及通用计算。例如，英特尔的至强处理器为谷歌云的C4和N4实例提供支持。

尽管谷歌基于Armv9架构的自研Axion处理器为其云服务带来了更高的控制力、更低的成本与能效，但其数据中心内仍有大量工作负载需要保持与x86架构的向后兼容性，或依赖英特尔至强处理器所提供的极致单线程性能。这种需求预计将持续多年，这是双方达成此项协议的核心原因。

为了进一步提升英特尔至强平台在超大规模数据中心中的效率与适用性，谷歌将与英特尔共同开发定制IPU，将网络、存储及安全功能从主CPU中卸载。最终，英特尔至强平台将结合x86架构的高单线程性能与定制化的基础设施处理能力，从而在谷歌高度定制化的环境中保持竞争力。

此消息发布之际，超大规模云服务商和人工智能平台开发商正加速采用基于Arm指令集架构的自研定制CPU。市场研究机构Counterpoint Research近期发布报告预测，到2029年，运行定制芯片的人工智能服务器中将有90%采用Arm架构，x86与RISC-V架构合计仅占约10%。英特尔与谷歌的联合声明则明确指出，配备定制IPU的至强CPU在未来数年仍将继续用于人工智能及其他高要求工作负载。

自21世纪初以来，英特尔至强处理器一直是云基础设施的基石，更早之前也已应用于谷歌的服务器。因此，x86架构，特别是至强处理器，不会在短期内退出谷歌的数据中心。此次合作公告再次明确了英特尔至强CPU在当下人工智能时代的相关性。尤其当这一肯定来自长期在其几乎所有服务中部署专用定制加速器的谷歌时，其信号意义被显著放大。

谷歌方面表示，英特尔是近二十年来值得信赖的合作伙伴，其至强处理器路线图使谷歌有信心持续满足工作负载对性能和效率日益增长的需求。这项合作也预示着，在未来异构计算架构中，通用CPU与定制加速单元（如IPU）的协同设计将成为满足复杂、多样化云与人工智能负载的关键模式。

history.title: Intel's EMIB-T packaging technology set for fab rollout this year — as TSMC CoWoS capacity remains limited,EMIB-T is preparing for advanced AI accelerator designs
history.description: If these “billions per year” in deals close, it’d mark quite the turnaround for Intel Foundry, which generated just $307 million in external revenue last year.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel CFO Dave Zinsner told the Morgan Stanley TMT conference last month that Intel Foundry is "close to closing some deals that are in the billions per year in terms of revenue" on advanced packaging alone, with EMIB-T, the next-generation variant of Intel's embedded bridge technology, driving customer interest as TSMC's CoWoS-L capacity remains structurally oversubscribed.That tech, EMIB-T, which adds through silicon vias (TSVs) to the bridge, is expected to enter production fab rollout this year and addresses the limitations that have kept standard EMIB out of high-power AI accelerator sockets: HBM 4 class power delivery and large package scaling.If these “billions per year” in deals close, it’d mark quite the turnaround for Intel Foundry, which generated just $307 million in external revenue last year against a $10.3 billion operating loss. Given that 18A isn’t expected to reach industry-standard yields until next year at the earliest, packaging is the fastest on-ramp for Intel, and EMIB-T is what could convert that into AI accelerator revenue. Intel Foundry's most recent announcement aligns well with this, having signed up to join Musk's Terafab project, though the reality of the effort delivering on all that's been promised remains extremely optimistic, as we've broken down.Article continues below You may like Intel reportedly in talks with Google and Amazon over advanced packaging Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push Intel shows off leading-edge tech with massive AI processor test vehicle EMIB-T and PSVs (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Standard EMIB, which has been in volume production since 2017, embeds small silicon bridge dies in cavities within an organic substrate to route signals horizontally between adjacent chiplets, avoiding the cost and reticle limits of a full silicon interposer.Intel deliberately skipped through-silicon vias entirely with EMIB, keeping the bridge die simple and cheap. That meant power had to be routed around the bridge via the organic substrate in long, resistive paths, limiting the current that could be delivered to the dies above. That ceiling was fine for the likes of Sapphire Rapids and Ponte Vecchio, but not for HBM4-class accelerators.EMIB-T reverses that decision by adding through-silicon vias (TSVs) to the bridge die. In doing so, EMIB-T enables vertical power delivery directly through the bridge, integrates Metal-Insulator-Metal capacitors for noise suppression, and adds a copper ground plane grid for signal isolation.Dr. Rahul Manepalli, Intel Fellow and VP of Substrate Packaging Development, gave us a look into the full specifications of EMIB-T at last May’s Electronic Components Technology Conference — a 45-micron bump pitch with a roadmap to 35- and 25-micron, energy efficiency of around 0.25 pJ/bit, and UCle-A interfaces running at 32 Gb/s of data transfer or higher per pin. EMIB-T supports HBM3, HBM3E, HBM4, and future HBM5 stacks, and scales to a 120mm x 180mm package supporting more than 38 bridges and over 12 reticle-sized dies.Intel then followed up just before Christmas with a conceptual 2.5D/3D package integrating 16 compute elements across eight base dies, 24 HBM5 stacks, and a 10,296 mm2 silicon footprint, scaling to 12x reticle size and beating TSMC's planned 9.5x CoWoS-L ceiling. For comparison, TSMC's CoWoS-L roadmap targets 5.5x reticle this year and 9.5x by 2027, while Intel is targeting 8x reticle in 2026 and 12x or more by 2028.Bernstein analysts estimate a substantial cost differential, with EMIB packaging running in the low hundreds of dollars per chip versus an estimated $900 to $1,000 for CoWoS on a Rubin-class accelerator, according to figures cited by Investing.com in recent packaging analysis. Intel also claims roughly 90% wafer utilization for bridge dies versus approximately 60% for large interposers.CoWoS-L bottlenecks (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)TSMC has expanded CoWoS capacity from approximately 35,000 wafers per month at the end of 2024 to roughly 80,000 by the end of 2025, with a target of around 130,000 by the end of this year through new facilities at AP7 in Chiayi and AP8 in Tainan. As a result of these aggressive ramp-ups, Counterpoint Research projects industry-wide advanced packaging capacity could expand by roughly 80% year-over-year in 2026. What to read next Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' Apple and Nvidia considering Intel for 2028 chip production, report claims AI chip design is pushing advanced chip packaging to its limits Nvidia secured more than 60% of TSMC's total CoWoS capacity for 2025 and 2026, with every Blackwell GPU and the upcoming Rubin architecture requiring CoWoS-L. At the back-end of 2025, it was reported that TSMC’s CoWoS capacity was stretched due to AI demand, and that Intel was picking up packaging interest from firms either blocked from accessing CoWoS or looking for a shorter path to production.TrendForce then reported in early December that both CoWoS-L and CoWoS-S were fully booked, and that Google had cut its 2026 TPU target by 1 million units due to allocation limits. TSMC CEO C.C. Wei, of course, said during the Q3 2025 earnings call that AI-related capacity, both front-end and back-end, remained very tight, falling “about three times short” of AI demand.Only a handful of customers have been publicly named as being interested in EMIB-T, and there’s been no official confirmation by Intel to date. MediaTek was named by the Commercial Times back in November as actively recruiting engineers with EMIB experience, with the publication adding that Intel was “tapping Intel’s advanced EMIB-T packaging” to secure more capacity. Amazon is reportedly another named customer, claiming Intel is in active discussions with AWS for Trainium-class custom AI processor packaging.Meanwhile, standard EMIB has the customer pipeline that EMIB-T doesn’t. Nvidia's $5 billion September 2025 investment in Intel includes confirmed use of EMIB and Foveros, with Jensen Huang and Lip-Bu Tan naming both technologies on the joint announcement call, and DigiTimes has reported that approximately 25% of Feynman GPU packaging will run through Intel. Microsoft's Maia AI accelerator is Intel Foundry's marquee 18A customer under a $15 billion lifetime contract originally announced at Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2024, while Google is understood to be designing EMIB into its 2027 TPU v9.Intel’s Foundry VP of Packaging, Mark Gardner, told EE Times way back in March last year that Intel is "in production today and have been in production even last year in cases where we took things that were designed for CoWoS and are now designed for manufacture with either EMIB or Foveros," confirming that live design migration off TSMC is already occurring on the standard EMIB platform.EMIB-T for Jaguar Shores?Clearwater Forest, the 288-core Xeon 6+ E-core server processor formally launched in H1 2026 after a delay from its original 2025 target, uses second-generation standard EMIB combined with Foveros Direct 3D hybrid bonding across a 17-tile package. The configuration spans 12 compute tiles on Intel 18A, three base tiles on Intel 3, and two I/O tiles on Intel 7, connected by 12 EMIB bridges. Diamond Rapids, the Xeon 7 P-core server processor with up to 192 cores, also targets H2 2026 using standard EMIB plus Foveros.Jaguar Shores, the successor to the canceled Falcon Shores AI accelerator, is the likely first Intel product to use EMIB-T. A leaked test chip showed a 92.5mm x 92.5mm package with four compute tiles and eight HBM4 interfaces on Intel 18A. That HBM4 integration is exactly the use case that standard EMIB will struggle with, making Jaguar Shores the natural first home for the variant. Panther Lake, Intel's first 18A client processor shipping in volume since late 2025, uses Foveros-S 2.5D rather than any EMIB variant.No EMIB-T customer production for a ‘year or two’Intel's packaging footprint spans three continents. In Rio Rancho, New Mexico, Intel launched the Fab 9 facility in January 2024 as the first co-located high-volume advanced packaging site, adjacent to the existing Fab 11X and backed by a $3.5 billion investment. Intel later received $500 million in CHIPS Act funding earmarked for further modernization of Fab 9, Fab 11, and Fab 11X.In Asia, the Penang, Malaysia, advanced packaging complex is 99% complete, with first-phase operations beginning sometime this year. Intel has also outsourced EMIB production to Amkor’s Songdo K5 facility in South Korea — described as a “first-ever” outsourcing move by TrendForce — with additional Amkor sites planned in Portugal and Arizona.However, EMIB-T itself hasn’t yet shipped in any commercial product. Standard EMIB has been in volume since 2017 and is what every named Intel packaging customer is currently using, but the TSV-enhanced variant is only now due to roll out, with external customer designs expected in production "in the next year or two," says Mark Gardner.

history.titleZh: 英特尔EMIB-T封装技术预计今年投产；台积电CoWoS产能紧张之际，EMIB-T正为AI加速器先进设计蓄力
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔首席财务官戴夫·津斯纳在上月摩根士丹利TMT会议上透露，英特尔代工服务（IFS）仅靠先进封装业务就“即将敲定几笔年均价值数十亿美元的交易”，其中下一代嵌入式桥接技术EMIB-T正推动客户兴趣增长，而台积电的CoWoS-L产能目前仍处于结构性供不应求状态。

这项名为EMIB-T的技术通过在桥接芯片中增加硅通孔（TSV），预计将于今年进入生产阶段，旨在解决标准EMIB技术无法应用于高功耗AI加速器的限制，包括满足HBM4级别的供电需求以及实现更大尺寸封装扩展。若这些年均“数十亿美元”的交易得以落实，将标志着英特尔代工业务的重大转折——该部门去年外部营收仅3.07亿美元，运营亏损却高达103亿美元。考虑到英特尔18A制程预计最早明年才能达到行业标准良率，先进封装成为其最快切入市场的路径，而EMIB-T正是将其转化为AI加速器营收的关键技术。

**技术原理与演进**
自2017年量产的标准EMIB技术将小型硅桥接芯片嵌入有机基板空腔中，实现相邻芯粒间的水平信号互连，避免了使用全硅中介层的成本和光罩尺寸限制。英特尔当时刻意在EMIB中完全避开硅通孔，以保持桥接芯片结构简单、成本低廉。但这也意味着电力必须通过有机基板绕开桥接芯片传输，路径长、电阻大，限制了传输至上方芯片的电流。这一限制对Sapphire Rapids和Ponte Vecchio等产品尚可接受，却无法满足HBM4级加速器的需求。

EMIB-T通过为桥接芯片添加硅通孔扭转了这一设计。该技术可实现垂直电力传输直接穿过桥接芯片，集成金属-绝缘体-金属电容以抑制噪声，并增加铜接地网格用于信号隔离。英特尔院士兼基板封装开发副总裁Rahul Manepalli博士在去年5月的电子元件技术会议上披露了EMIB-T完整规格：凸点间距45微米（路线图规划35/25微米），能效约0.25皮焦/比特，UCle-A接口每引脚数据传输速率达32Gb/s或更高。该技术支持HBM3、HBM3E、HBM4及未来HBM5堆栈，可扩展至120mm×180mm封装尺寸，容纳超过38个桥接芯片和12个以上光罩尺寸的芯粒。

**性能与成本优势**
去年圣诞节前，英特尔进一步展示了集成16个计算单元、24个HBM5堆栈、硅面积达10,296平方毫米的2.5D/3D概念封装方案，尺寸达12倍光罩，超越台积电计划中9.5倍光罩的CoWoS-L上限。对比显示，台积电CoWoS-L路线图目标为今年达5.5倍光罩、2027年达9.5倍光罩，而英特尔目标为2026年达8倍光罩、2028年达12倍或更大。

伯恩斯坦分析师估算存在显著成本差异：EMIB封装每芯片成本为数百美元低区间，而据Investing.com近期封装分析引用的数据，CoWoS在Rubin级加速器上的成本约900-1000美元。英特尔还宣称其桥接芯片晶圆利用率约90%，远高于大型中介层约60%的利用率。

**产能竞争格局**
台积电已将CoWoS月产能从2024年底的约3.5万片晶圆提升至2025年底的约8万片，并通过嘉义AP7和台南AP8新设施计划在今年底达到约13万片。Counterpoint Research预测，2026年全球先进封装产能可能同比增长约80%。然而，英伟达已锁定台积电2025-2026年超60%的CoWoS总产能，且每颗Blackwell GPU及即将推出的Rubin架构都需要CoWoS-L封装。2025年下半年有报道称，台积电CoWoS产能因AI需求紧绷，英特尔正承接那些无法获得CoWoS产能或寻求更短生产路径的公司的封装订单。

TrendForce去年12月初报告指出，CoWoS-L和CoWoS-S产能均已满载，谷歌因分配限制将2026年TPU目标产量削减100万颗。台积电CEO魏哲家在2025年第三季度财报电话会议中承认，AI相关的前后端产能仍然非常紧张，“约为AI需求的三分之一”。

**客户生态与迁移案例**
目前仅有少数客户被公开提及对EMIB-T感兴趣。去年11月，《工商时报》报道联发科正积极招募具有EMIB经验的工程师，并称英特尔正利用EMIB-T争取更多产能。亚马逊也被报道是另一个潜在客户，据称英特尔正与AWS就Trainium级定制AI处理器封装进行洽谈。

与此同时，标准EMIB已建立成熟的客户渠道。英伟达2025年9月对英特尔的50亿美元投资已确认将使用EMIB和Foveros技术，黄仁勋与陈立武在联合发布会上点名了这两项技术。Digitimes报道称约25%的Feynman GPU封装将通过英特尔完成。微软的Maia AI加速器是英特尔代工18A制程的旗舰客户，双方签署了150亿美元终身合约；而谷歌据悉正将EMIB设计纳入其2027年TPU v9。

英特尔代工封装副总裁Mark Gardner去年3月对《EE Times》表示：“我们现在已投入生产，甚至在去年就已开始生产那些原本为CoWoS设计、现改用EMIB或Foveros制造的产品。”这证实基于标准EMIB平台从台积电迁移设计的进程已在推进。

**产品应用路线**
Clearwater Forest（288核Xeon 6+ E核服务器处理器）采用第二代标准EMIB结合Foveros Direct 3D混合键合，在17芯粒封装中通过12个EMIB桥接。Diamond Rapids（最高192核Xeon 7 P核服务器处理器）也计划使用标准EMIB加Foveros。

而很可能首发搭载EMIB-T的英特尔产品是Jaguar Shores（已取消的Falcon Shores AI加速器后继者）。一款泄露的测试芯片显示其采用92.5mm×92.5mm封装，集成4个计算芯粒和8个HBM4接口——这恰恰是标准EMIB难以应对的用例，使Jaguar Shores成为该变体技术的自然首秀平台。值得注意的是，英特尔首款18A客户端处理器Panther Lake采用Foveros-S 2.5D而非任何EMIB变体。

**产能布局与时间表**
英特尔的封装产能遍布三大洲：2024年1月在新墨西哥州Rio Rancho启动的Fab 9是首个同址大规模先进封装基地，毗邻现有Fab 11X，获得35亿美元投资支持，随后又收到5亿美元CHIPS法案资金用于Fab 9/11/11X现代化改造。在亚洲，马来西亚槟城先进封装综合体已完成99%，第一阶段运营将于今年开始。英特尔还将EMIB生产外包给Amkor韩国Songdo K5工厂（TrendForce称此为“首次”外包），并计划在葡萄牙和亚利桑那州增设Amkor生产点。

然而，EMIB-T尚未在任何商用产品中出货。标准EMIB自2017年起量产，当前所有已公布的英特尔封装客户均在使用该版本，而增强TSV的变体技术刚刚开始推广。Mark Gardner表示，外部客户基于EMIB-T的设计预计“未来一两年内”投入生产。

history.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
history.description:
Today on Decoder, let’s talk about the looming AI monetization cliff, and whether some of the biggest companies in the space can become real, profitable businesses before they careen right off it.
My guest today is Hayden Field, who’s our senior AI reporter here at The Verge. She’s been keeping close tabs on both Anthropic and OpenAI, and how these two companies in particular tell us a whole lot about the AI industry in 2026. 
You’ve certainly heard a version of the monetization cliff story before. The biggest AI firms are built off the back of hundreds of billions in capital investment, and they’re linked to even greater amounts of forward-looking investment in data center build-out, chips, and other infrastructure spend. At some point, the profits have to materialize, or the bubble pops. Maybe AGI arrives, maybe the economy crashes, who knows. 
You’ve heard me ask some version of this question to scores of CEOs here on this show, and a majority of them have hinted toward the bubble popping — they think some companies will fail in spectacular fashion, some will succeed, and the opportunities, especially the money, are simply too big to ignore. We’re doing this, whether we want to or not — the market depends on it. 



Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here. 
So these last few weeks have felt like a very important inflection point, as both Anthropic and OpenAI have started to react to the reality of needing to go public — needing to make money, 
The catalyst for this change is AI agents, and products like Claude Code and Cowork, as well as the open-source OpenClaw and OpenAI’s Codex, have radically changed how these companies are thinking about their resources. And this is starting to affect how they behave — the products they support or suddenly kill, the restrictions they impose on customers, and the money they’re willing to burn toward their next big milestone. 
That’s because agents are valuable to customers right now, but agents also use far more compute. So the way people are using agents is burning tokens at a rate way faster than these companies anticipated, and that’s causing them to make hard decisions. 
We saw this most evidently last month when OpenAI abruptly killed its video-generation app Sora, ditching a $1 billion Disney licensing deal in the process. Why? It costs too much to run, and OpenAI needs the compute for Codex. We saw it again just last week, when Anthropic decided it would no longer let Claude users burn through compute resources using the OpenClaw agent framework through a standard subscription plan, instead forcing those users onto pay-as-you-go plans, which cost substantially more. 
As you’ll hear Hayden explain here, these are glimmers of a make-or-break moment for the AI industry, as both Anthropic and OpenAI barrel toward two of the biggest IPOs in history. And the pressure on these companies to make money has never been this intense. 
The projections these companies have made, which just this week were leaked to the Wall Street Journal, tell a story of mind-boggling growth, to the tune of hundreds of billions in revenue and profitability by the end of the decade. But the most important questions now are can the AI companies pull this off, and what compromises will they make to reach that goal and avoid crashing and burning? 
Okay: Verge senior policy reporter Hayden Field on the AI monetization cliff and the race to profitability. Here we go.


If you’d like to read more about what we discussed in this episode, check out these links:
The vibes are off at OpenAI | The Verge
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude | The Verge
Why OpenAI killed Sora | The Verge
OpenAI just bought TBPN | The Verge
National poll shows voters like AI less than ICE | The Verge
The spiraling cost of making AI | WSJ
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo taking leave amid exec shake-up | Wired
OpenAI raises another $122B at $850B valuation | The Verge
Questions or comments about this episode? Hit us up at decoder@theverge.com. We really do read every email!

history.titleZh: 现在AI行业的利润追逐战关乎存亡。
history.descriptionZh:
近期，AI行业正面临迫在眉睫的“盈利悬崖”——尽管顶级AI公司已获得数千亿美元资本投入，并承诺对数据中心、芯片等基础设施进行更大规模投资，但盈利压力已到达临界点。若无法实现可持续的商业模式，行业泡沫可能破裂。在这一背景下，Anthropic与OpenAI两家代表性企业的动向尤为关键，它们不仅预示着2026年AI产业的走向，更映射出整个行业在规模化盈利前的挣扎与抉择。

**核心技术动因：AI代理引发资源消耗危机**  
引发当前局面的直接技术因素是**AI代理（AI Agents）**的普及。例如Anthropic的Claude Code、Cowork，开源框架OpenClaw，以及OpenAI的Codex等产品，正在改变企业分配计算资源的方式。这些代理能够执行复杂多步任务，但同时也消耗远超预期的计算资源（token消耗速度远超公司预测），导致运营成本急剧上升。这迫使企业重新评估产品策略：支持高价值代理服务的同时，不得不削减或关闭其他高成本项目。

**战略调整案例：成本控制下的产品收缩**  
- **OpenAI关闭Sora视频生成应用**：尽管已与迪士尼达成10亿美元授权协议，但因Sora运行成本过高，OpenAI选择终止该项目，将计算资源集中投向Codex等代理相关产品。  
- **Anthropic限制OpenClaw访问**：近期Anthropic禁止标准订阅用户通过Claude使用OpenClaw框架，强制将其迁移至按量计费模式，显著提高使用门槛以控制资源消耗。

**财务压力与上市倒计时**  
两家公司均处于筹备历史上规模最大IPO的关键阶段，盈利压力空前。据《华尔街日报》泄露的财务预测，它们预计到2030年将实现数千亿美元营收与盈利。然而，实现该目标需要平衡两个矛盾：一方面必须扩大代理等高价值服务，另一方面需严格控制爆炸式增长的计算成本。当前举措——如收缩业务、调整订阅策略——正是其应对“盈利悬崖”的早期信号。

**行业影响与未来挑战**  
这一转折点揭示出AI行业普遍困境：  
1. **资本依赖与盈利路径的冲突**：巨额前期投资需通过规模化商用兑现，但现有技术栈（尤其是大模型推理）的成本结构尚未优化至可盈利水平。  
2. **客户行为改变商业模型**：用户对代理的高频使用正在打破企业原有的资源规划模型，迫使企业重新设计计费体系与服务边界。  
3. **技术决策与商业目标的捆绑**：公司不再单纯追求技术突破，而是依据资源消耗与盈利潜力决定产品存续，技术路线日益服从于财务指标。

**应用场景的重塑**  
短期内，面向企业的代理服务（如代码生成、复杂流程自动化）将成为营收焦点，因其能直接提升客户生产力并支持更高定价。而面向消费者的轻量级应用（如创意生成工具）可能因成本受限而收缩。同时，行业或将出现更多“分层服务”模式：基础功能维持订阅制，高阶代理服务则全面转向按量计费。

当前Anthropic与OpenAI的抉择仅是开端。随着更多AI公司逼近盈利临界点，行业将进入残酷的筛选阶段——唯有在技术价值、成本控制与商业化节奏间找到平衡的企业，才可能跨越“盈利悬崖”，真正成长为可持续的商业模式。否则，资本泡沫破裂与技术收缩的连锁反应将不可避免。

history.title: Intel and SambaNova team up on heterogenous AI inference platform — different hardware performs different workloads
history.description: Intel and SambaNova announce heterogeneous inference platform that can take advantage of Intel Xeon 6 CPUs, SambaNova SN50 RDUs, and Nvidia GPUs.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel and SambaNova on Wednesday announced their joint production-ready heterogeneous inference architecture that relies on AI accelerators or GPUs for prefill, SambaNova reconfigurable dataflow units (RDUs) SN50 for decode, and Xeon 6 processors for agentic tools and system orchestration. The platform is designed to address as broad a set of workloads as possible to siphon some of the market share away from Nvidia and other emerging players.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe heterogeneous inference platform by Intel and SambaNova separates inference into distinct stages handled by different silicon: It uses AI GPUs or AI accelerators for ingesting long prompts and building key-value caches; SambaNova's SN50 RDU for decoding and generating tokens; and Xeon 6 processors for running agent-related operations (e.g., compiling and executing code and validating outputs) as well as coordinating and distributing workloads across hardware. Splitting prefill, decode, and token generation stages is similar to Nvidia's approach to its Rubin platform, which is based on the Rubin CPX and heavy-duty Rubin GPU with HBM4 memory — with the obvious difference that the Rubin CPX is not coming to market. But, more importantly for Intel, the new platform will rely on its Xeon 6 processors — not on competing offerings. (Image credit: SambaNova)The solution is scheduled to be available in the second half of 2026 to enterprises, cloud operators, and sovereign AI programs seeking scalable inference platforms in general and coding agents, and other agentic workloads in particular, completely in-house.According to SambaNova's internal data, Xeon 6 achieves over 50% faster LLVM compilation compared to Arm-based server CPUs, and delivers up to 70% higher performance in vector database workloads, relative to competing x86 processors — namely, AMD EPYC. These gains are intended to shorten end-to-end development cycles for coding agents and similar applications, the two companies claim.Perhaps the biggest advantage of the joint production-ready heterogeneous inference architecture is that SambaNova SN50 and Xeon-based servers are drop-in compatible with data centers that can handle 30kW — which is the vast majority of enterprise data centers. "The data center software ecosystem is built on x86, and it runs on Xeon — providing a mature, proven foundation that developers, enterprises, and cloud providers rely on at scale," said Kevork Kechichian, Executive Vice President and General Manager of the Data Center Group (DCG) at Intel Corporation. "Workloads of the future will require a heterogeneous mix of computing, and this collaboration with SambaNova delivers a cost‑efficient, high‑performance inference architecture designed to meet customer needs at scale — powered by Xeon 6."Article continues below You may like Sambanova introduces new AI accelerator, partners with Intel to deploy Xeon CPUs for inferencing and agentic workloads Intel's roadmap adds mysterious 'hybrid' AI processor featuring x86 CPUs, dedicated AI accelerator, and programmable IP Intel Xeon 6 selected as host CPU for Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 英特尔携手SambaNova共推异构AI推理平台——差异化硬件运行不同工作负载
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔与AI加速器公司SambaNova于本周三联合宣布，推出其生产就绪的异构推理架构。该平台旨在通过结合不同硬件的优势，处理尽可能广泛的工作负载，以从英伟达及其他新兴竞争者手中争夺市场份额。

**背景与核心架构**
该异构推理平台的核心思想是将AI推理过程，特别是大语言模型（LLM）的推理，分解为不同阶段，并由专用硬件处理，以实现整体效率最大化。具体分工如下：
1.  **预填充（Prefill）阶段**：由AI GPU或AI加速器负责。此阶段处理长提示输入，并构建关键的键值（KV）缓存，为后续生成做好准备。
2.  **解码（Decode）与令牌生成阶段**：由SambaNova的**SN50可重构数据流单元（RDU）** 负责。这是生成文本输出的核心阶段。
3.  **智能体工具与系统协调阶段**：由英特尔的**至强6（Xeon 6）处理器**负责。它运行与AI智能体相关的操作（如编译和执行代码、验证输出），并负责在整套硬件间协调和分发工作负载。

这种将预填充、解码与生成分离的思路，与英伟达基于Rubin CPX和Rubin GPU的Rubin平台策略相似。然而，关键区别在于，英特尔- SambaNova方案的核心协调与智能体处理单元是实际即将上市的至强6处理器，而非尚未面市的Rubin CPX。

**性能优势**
根据SambaNova的内部数据，至强6处理器在特定工作负载上展现出显著优势：
*   与基于Arm架构的服务器CPU相比，在LLVM编译任务上快**50%以上**。
*   与竞争x86处理器（即AMD EPYC）相比，在向量数据库工作负载上性能提升高达**70%**。
这些性能提升旨在缩短编码智能体（Coding Agent）等应用的端到端开发周期。

**技术影响与部署前景**
该解决方案计划于**2026年下半年**向企业、云运营商以及寻求构建主权AI的机构提供，特别适合那些需要可扩展推理平台，并专注于编码智能体和其他智能体工作负载的客户。

其一大突出优势在于**部署便利性**：SambaNova SN50加速卡与基于至强处理器的服务器，能够“即插即用”地兼容绝大多数现有企业数据中心（支持30kW功率规格），无需大规模改造基础设施。英特尔数据中心事业部总经理Kevork Kechichian指出，数据中心软件生态系统本就建立在x86架构并以至强为基础，这为开发者与企业提供了一个成熟、可靠的规模化部署基石。

**总结**
英特尔与SambaNova的合作，标志着面向复杂AI工作负载（尤其是智能体应用）的异构计算架构正加速迈向成熟商用。通过将GPU/加速器、专用AI计算单元（RDU）和通用CPU进行任务级优化整合，该平台试图在性能、能效和现有生态兼容性之间取得平衡，为挑战英伟达在AI推理领域的统治地位提供了一条差异化路径。至强6处理器被定位为未来异构计算混合负载中的系统“大脑”与协调中心，确保了英特尔在AI数据中心的核心地位。
